But now, despite the very important
position he held, he knew well enough that, compared with Genji, he was
in popular estimation a very insignificant person indeed.
pairs.quijote_source:
Dead at thy feet shouldst see me lying, ere
My heart repented of its love for thee.

pairs.display: When Suzaku gave his children toys, it was always Nyosan who had the first choice, to take your own life, or Lothario’s?
pairs.genji_half: When Suzaku gave his children toys, it was always Nyosan who had the first choice,
pairs.quijote_half: to take your own life, or Lothario’s?
pairs.genji_source:
When Suzaku gave his children toys, it
was always Nyosan who had the first choice, the others never getting
the smallest trinket unless she had first refused it.
pairs.quijote_source:
Can it be that you mean to take your own
life, or Lothario’s?

pairs.display: But he was determined, next time an opportunity occurred, simply to repeat to Genji what Kashiwagi had said, for giving such authority in his house to this shameless fellow!
pairs.genji_half: But he was determined, next time an opportunity occurred, simply to repeat to Genji what Kashiwagi had said,
pairs.quijote_half: for giving such authority in his house to this shameless fellow!
pairs.genji_source:
But he was determined, next time an opportunity
occurred, simply to repeat to Genji what Kashiwagi had said, and see
whether Genji could make sense of it all, or no.
pairs.quijote_source:
Ill betide my
master, Anselmo, for giving such authority in his house to this shameless
fellow!

pairs.display: It was whispered that all this trouble had arisen from Yoshikiyo’s indiscreet eloquence upon the occasion hoodwinked man there could be in the world.
pairs.genji_half: It was whispered that all this trouble had arisen from Yoshikiyo’s indiscreet eloquence upon the occasion
pairs.quijote_half: hoodwinked man there could be in the world.
pairs.genji_source:
It was whispered that all this
trouble had arisen from Yoshikiyo’s indiscreet eloquence upon the
occasion when after Genji’s cure they had climbed the mountain summit
and looked down towards the western seas.
pairs.quijote_source:
And so Anselmo was left the most charmingly hoodwinked man there could be
in the world.

pairs.display: Ukon herself was the bearer of this letter; she also reported much of what Genji had said to her, and threatened to stab Leonela, bidding her tell the truth or he would kill her.
pairs.genji_half: Ukon herself was the bearer of this letter; she also reported much of what Genji had said to her,
pairs.quijote_half: and threatened to stab Leonela, bidding her tell the truth or he would kill her.
pairs.genji_source:
Ukon herself was the bearer of this letter; she also reported much of
what Genji had said to her, especially such expressions of cordiality
and goodwill as would tend to allay Tamakatsura’s apprehensions.
pairs.quijote_source:
Anselmo would not believe it, but blind with rage drew a dagger and
threatened to stab Leonela, bidding her tell the truth or he would kill
her.

pairs.display: They were beginning to discuss the affairs of Iyo’s province to utter a word in reply, still less to decide upon what he should do.
pairs.genji_half: They were beginning to discuss the affairs of Iyo’s province
pairs.quijote_half: to utter a word in reply, still less to decide upon what he should do.
pairs.genji_source:
They were
beginning to discuss the affairs of Iyo’s province and Genji was even
joking with him, when a sudden feeling of embarrassment came over him.
pairs.quijote_source:
The state of perplexity to which Camilla reduced
Lothario was such that he was unable to utter a word in reply, still less
to decide upon what he should do.

pairs.display: For a moment Genji’s servants who were preceding him to the Heir Apparent’s rooms blocked his path but not finding her in bed or anywhere in the house he was lost in amazement.
pairs.genji_half: For a moment Genji’s servants who were preceding him to the Heir Apparent’s rooms blocked his path
pairs.quijote_half: but not finding her in bed or anywhere in the house he was lost in amazement.
pairs.genji_source:
For a moment Genji’s servants who were
preceding him to the Heir Apparent’s rooms blocked his path and forced
him to stand waiting till they had passed.
pairs.quijote_source:
He returned,
uneasy, to tell Camilla, but not finding her in bed or anywhere in the
house he was lost in amazement.

pairs.display: His presence was indeed becoming in every way more and more imperative, but of his life.
pairs.genji_half: His presence was indeed becoming in every way more and more imperative,
pairs.quijote_half: but of his life.
pairs.genji_source:
His presence was indeed becoming in every way more
and more imperative, and at last the Emperor decided to recall him,
whether Kōkiden approved or not.
pairs.quijote_source:
This disastrous intelligence almost robbed Anselmo not only of his senses
but of his life.

pairs.display: But recently the occasion of Lady Fujitsubo’s death had called him back to the Court, and shortly afterwards died, worn out by grief and melancholy.
pairs.genji_half: But recently the occasion of Lady Fujitsubo’s death had called him back to the Court,

pairs.display: How simple it would all have been if she could merely have walked across to Murasaki’s domain and covering his head a black Milanese bonnet, and his snow-white beard fell below his girdle.
pairs.genji_half: How simple it would all have been if she could merely have walked across to Murasaki’s domain
pairs.quijote_half: and covering his head a black Milanese bonnet, and his snow-white beard fell below his girdle.
pairs.genji_source:
How simple it would all
have been if she could merely have walked across to Murasaki’s domain
when the fancy seized her, enjoyed herself among the flowers and gone
away!
pairs.quijote_source:
On his shoulders
and breast he had a green satin collegiate hood, and covering his head a
black Milanese bonnet, and his snow-white beard fell below his girdle.

pairs.display: Here they picked up Murasaki’s ladies, the valiant Roman emperor, what was the best death.
pairs.genji_half: Here they picked up Murasaki’s ladies,
pairs.quijote_half: the valiant Roman emperor, what was the best death.
pairs.genji_source:
Here they picked up Murasaki’s ladies, who
were waiting at the Pavilion by appointment.
pairs.quijote_source:
They asked Julius Caesar, the valiant Roman
emperor, what was the best death.

pairs.display: At Murasaki’s request an offering of flowers with his finger on his lip, approaches Melisendra from behind?
pairs.genji_half: At Murasaki’s request an offering of flowers
pairs.quijote_half: with his finger on his lip, approaches Melisendra from behind?
pairs.genji_source:
At Murasaki’s request an offering of flowers was to
be made to the presiding Buddha.
pairs.quijote_source:
Do you not see that Moor, who silently
and stealthily, with his finger on his lip, approaches Melisendra from
behind?

pairs.display: The boys arrived by water, and in joy and gladness take the road to Paris.
pairs.genji_half: The boys arrived by water,
pairs.quijote_half: and in joy and gladness take the road to Paris.
pairs.genji_source:
The boys arrived by water,
having embarked at the landing-stage in front of Murasaki’s rooms.
pairs.quijote_source:
You see how they wheel round and quit the city, and in joy and
gladness take the road to Paris.

pairs.display: On looking back, I see that I have forgotten to mention the presents and killing and destroying, but only little pasteboard figures!

pairs.quijote_half: and killing and destroying, but only little pasteboard figures!
pairs.genji_source:
On looking back, I see that I have forgotten to mention the presents
which Murasaki distributed among her visitors after the ceremonies
of the day before.
pairs.quijote_source:
can’t you see they’re not real Moors you’re
knocking down and killing and destroying, but only little pasteboard
figures!

pairs.display: The finish of the course was not so very far from Murasaki’s rooms, was as much astonished at his mad freaks as at his generosity.
pairs.genji_half: The finish of the course was not so very far from Murasaki’s rooms,
pairs.quijote_half: was as much astonished at his mad freaks as at his generosity.
pairs.genji_source:
The finish of the course was not so very far from Murasaki’s
rooms, so that her gentlewomen too were able to get some idea of what
was going on.
pairs.quijote_source:
The landlord, who did not know Don
Quixote, was as much astonished at his mad freaks as at his generosity.

pairs.display: Murasaki, who had first taken to reading romances in order to see whether they were suitable draw their swords, and risk their persons, lives, and properties.
pairs.genji_half: Murasaki, who had first taken to reading romances in order to see whether they were suitable
pairs.quijote_half: draw their swords, and risk their persons, lives, and properties.
pairs.genji_source:
Murasaki, who had first taken to reading romances in order to see
whether they were suitable for her adopted daughter, the Princess from
Akashi, was now deeply immersed in them.

pairs.display: The book seemed as old-fashioned to Murasaki for one single step further thou shalt not make in my company.
pairs.genji_half: The book seemed as old-fashioned to Murasaki
pairs.quijote_half: for one single step further thou shalt not make in my company.
pairs.genji_source:
The book seemed as old-fashioned to Murasaki as Hannah
        More’s novels do to us.
pairs.quijote_source:
Turn the rein, or the
halter, of thy Dapple, and begone home; for one single step further thou
shalt not make in my company.

pairs.display: When he reached the door at the end of the passage and most ludicrous figure that could be imagined.
pairs.genji_half: When he reached the door at the end of the passage
pairs.quijote_half: and most ludicrous figure that could be imagined.
pairs.genji_source:
When he reached the door at the
end of the passage he noticed that the great double-doors leading into
Murasaki’s room were half-open.
pairs.quijote_source:
She did so, and Don
Quixote was left the strangest and most ludicrous figure that could be
imagined.

pairs.genji_half: His father was bending over Murasaki
pairs.quijote_half: who was one of the listeners.
pairs.genji_source:
His father was bending over Murasaki
and whispering something in her ear; now he was laughing.
pairs.quijote_source:
exclaimed Dona Rodriguez, the duenna, who
was one of the listeners.

His father answered the
greeting and presently joined him, saying to Murasaki as he left the
room something which evidently referred to the imperfectly fastened
passage-door.
pairs.quijote_source:
In the dim caverns of the gloomy Dis,
Where, tracing mystic lines and characters,
My soul abideth now, there came to me

pairs.genji_half: On his return to Murasaki’s rooms,
pairs.quijote_half: along which the Distressed One advanced, Trifaldin still holding her hand.
pairs.genji_source:
On his return to Murasaki’s rooms, he found all the shutters
unbarred.
pairs.quijote_source:
The twelve duennas halted and formed a lane, along which the
Distressed One advanced, Trifaldin still holding her hand.

pairs.display: But meanwhile the girl’s reputation had, and such a perfection of beauty, that nature could not raise it higher.
pairs.genji_half: But meanwhile the girl’s reputation had,
pairs.quijote_half: and such a perfection of beauty, that nature could not raise it higher.
pairs.genji_source:
But meanwhile the girl’s reputation had, true to
Murasaki’s prediction, already begun to suffer.
pairs.quijote_source:
Time
passed, and the young Antonomasia reached the age of fourteen, and such a
perfection of beauty, that nature could not raise it higher.

pairs.display: If he had not openly accepted her as his concubine, was at that time in vogue in Kandy, which they call seguidillas!
pairs.genji_half: If he had not openly accepted her as his concubine,
pairs.quijote_half: was at that time in vogue in Kandy, which they call seguidillas!
pairs.genji_source:
If he had not
openly accepted her as his concubine, it was through fear of Murasaki
and the rest.
pairs.quijote_source:
And then, when they condescend to
compose a sort of verse that was at that time in vogue in Kandy, which
they call seguidillas!

pairs.display: His wife, an elder sister of Murasaki, Malambruno, for an enchanter and a giant!
pairs.genji_half: His wife, an elder sister of Murasaki,
pairs.quijote_half: Malambruno, for an enchanter and a giant!
pairs.genji_source:
His wife, an elder sister of Murasaki,
was four years his senior.
pairs.quijote_source:
A
thousand devils--not to curse thee--take thee, Malambruno, for an
enchanter and a giant!

pairs.display: My father says that, after all, Lady Murasaki is my own sister, was made by Merlin.
pairs.genji_half: My father says that, after all, Lady Murasaki is my own sister,
pairs.quijote_half: was made by Merlin.
pairs.genji_source:
My father says that, after all, Lady
Murasaki is my own sister, and it is very wrong of her to spoil my
happiness by introducing you to this girl.
pairs.quijote_source:
This horse, according to ancient tradition, was made by
Merlin.

pairs.display: He left Murasaki’s domain Didst thou the light mid Libya’s sands Or Jaca’s rocks first see? Did scaly serpents give thee suck?
pairs.genji_half: He left Murasaki’s domain
pairs.quijote_half: Didst thou the light mid Libya’s sands Or Jaca’s rocks first see? Did scaly serpents give thee suck?
pairs.genji_source:
He left Murasaki’s domain and made his way to the western
garden.
pairs.quijote_source:
Say, valiant youth, and so may God
    Thy enterprises speed,
Didst thou the light mid Libya’s sands
    Or Jaca’s rocks first see?

Did scaly serpents give thee suck?

pairs.display: Meanwhile Murasaki expressed a desire Or gloomy mountain cave?
pairs.genji_half: Meanwhile Murasaki expressed a desire
pairs.quijote_half: Or gloomy mountain cave?
pairs.genji_source:
Meanwhile Murasaki expressed
a desire to visit the August Birthplace.
pairs.quijote_source:
Wert cradled in the forest rude,
    Or gloomy mountain cave?

pairs.display: The visit to the Shrine was made very early on the morning of the Festival, From Manzanares to Genil, From Duero to Arlanza.
pairs.genji_half: The visit to the Shrine was made very early on the morning of the Festival,
pairs.quijote_half: From Manzanares to Genil, From Duero to Arlanza.
pairs.genji_source:
The visit to the Shrine
was made very early on the morning of the Festival, and Murasaki
was back in time to view the processions from the usual Stand.
pairs.quijote_source:
And she for this shall famous be
    From Tagus to Jarama,
From Manzanares to Genil,
    From Duero to Arlanza.

pairs.display: On the night of the actual Presentation Murasaki accompanied the child and looked upon their governor as another Solomon.
pairs.genji_half: On the night of the actual Presentation Murasaki accompanied the child
pairs.quijote_half: and looked upon their governor as another Solomon.
pairs.genji_source:
On the night of the actual Presentation Murasaki accompanied the child
in the hand-litter which was to convey her to the Eastern Palace.
pairs.quijote_source:
All were filled with amazement, and looked upon their
governor as another Solomon.

pairs.display: While dressing the little Princess in all the finery that this trying occasion demanded, he came riding on the hours, and morning very soon arrived.
pairs.genji_half: While dressing the little Princess in all the finery that this trying occasion demanded,
pairs.quijote_half: he came riding on the hours, and morning very soon arrived.
pairs.genji_source:
While
dressing the little Princess in all the finery that this trying
occasion demanded, Murasaki could not help passionately wishing that
this lovely child were really hers.
pairs.quijote_source:
But as Time is
fleet and no obstacle can stay his course, he came riding on the hours,
and morning very soon arrived.

pairs.display: It was not an easy conversation to get started, but Murasaki’s manner was so obviously kindly Having always work to do, To the poison Love instilleth Is the antidote most sure.
pairs.genji_half: It was not an easy conversation to get started, but Murasaki’s manner was so obviously kindly
pairs.quijote_half: Having always work to do, To the poison Love instilleth Is the antidote most sure.
pairs.genji_source:
It
was not an easy conversation to get started, but Murasaki’s manner was
so obviously kindly and sympathetic that a friendship was soon struck
up between them.
pairs.quijote_source:
Sewing, stitching, any labour,
  Having always work to do,
To the poison Love instilleth
  Is the antidote most sure.

pairs.display: For though generous and long-suffering, Modesty their highest praise.
pairs.genji_half: For though generous and long-suffering,
pairs.quijote_half: Modesty their highest praise.
pairs.genji_source:
For though generous and
long-suffering, Murasaki was capable of making judgments that were by
no means devoid of sharpness.
pairs.quijote_source:
And to proper-minded maidens
  Who desire the matron’s name
Modesty’s a marriage portion,
  Modesty their highest praise.

pairs.display: The discovery came as a cruel blow, yet even so there was no reason With the wanton damsels dally, But the modest take to wife.
pairs.genji_half: The discovery came as a cruel blow, yet even so there was no reason
pairs.quijote_half: With the wanton damsels dally, But the modest take to wife.
pairs.genji_source:
The discovery came as a cruel blow, yet even so
there was no reason to suppose that the new-comer was likely in any
sense to take Murasaki’s place.
pairs.quijote_source:
Men of prudence and discretion,
  Courtiers gay and gallant knights,
With the wanton damsels dally,
  But the modest take to wife.

pairs.display: While he knocked at the outer door of Murasaki’s room, at four in the morning.
pairs.genji_half: While he knocked at the outer door of Murasaki’s room,
pairs.quijote_half: at four in the morning.
pairs.genji_source:
While he knocked at the outer door
of Murasaki’s room, he murmured to himself those lines of Po Chū-i:
pairs.quijote_source:
From this place, the Sixteenth of August, at four in the
morning.

pairs.display: He came to Murasaki’s bedside but some knave sent here from hell to tempt me.
pairs.genji_half: He came to Murasaki’s bedside
pairs.quijote_half: but some knave sent here from hell to tempt me.
pairs.genji_source: He came to Murasaki’s bedside and pulled back the coverlet.
pairs.quijote_source:
You’re not from Miguelturra, but some knave sent here from hell
to tempt me.

pairs.display: But a moment later, looking at Murasaki, and a few years later went, no doubt, to heaven, for they were excellent good Catholic Christians.
pairs.genji_half: But a moment later, looking at Murasaki,
pairs.quijote_half: and a few years later went, no doubt, to heaven, for they were excellent good Catholic Christians.
pairs.genji_source:
But a moment later, looking
at Murasaki, he wondered how it could ever have occurred to him as
possible to leave her.
pairs.quijote_source:
My parents left me in service and returned
to their own country, and a few years later went, no doubt, to heaven,
for they were excellent good Catholic Christians.

pairs.display: The answer was long in coming, though it might be in contravention of all the aphorisms of Hippocrates.
pairs.genji_half: The answer was long in coming,
pairs.quijote_half: though it might be in contravention of all the aphorisms of Hippocrates.
pairs.genji_source:
The answer was long in coming, and tired of waiting
he went back to Murasaki in the inner room.
pairs.quijote_source:
At last Doctor
Pedro Recio Agilers of Tirteafuera promised to let him have supper that
night though it might be in contravention of all the aphorisms of
Hippocrates.

pairs.display: Alas, her writing was still quite unformed, the governor had supper.
pairs.genji_half: Alas, her writing was still quite unformed,
pairs.quijote_half: the governor had supper.
pairs.genji_source:
Alas, her writing was still quite unformed,
and he wished that it had been possible to prevent Murasaki from
seeing it some while longer.
pairs.quijote_source:
Night came, and with the permission of Doctor Pedro Recio, the governor
had supper.

pairs.display: If it had come from any one else he would certainly have discussed than a thousand reals in that gambling house opposite, and God knows how.
pairs.genji_half: If it had come from any one else he would certainly have discussed
pairs.quijote_half: than a thousand reals in that gambling house opposite, and God knows how.
pairs.genji_source:
If it had come from any one else he would
certainly have discussed this strangely backward letter with Murasaki
and attempted to account for its inefficiency.
pairs.quijote_source:
Your worship must know that this gentleman has just now won more
than a thousand reals in that gambling house opposite, and God knows how.

pairs.display: However, this time it was Murasaki herself who had suggested the meeting, and Sanchica cutting a rasher of bacon to be paved with eggs for his dinner.
pairs.genji_half: However, this time it was Murasaki herself who had suggested the meeting,
pairs.quijote_half: and Sanchica cutting a rasher of bacon to be paved with eggs for his dinner.
pairs.genji_source:
However, this time
it was Murasaki herself who had suggested the meeting, and there could
be no sense in opposing it.
pairs.quijote_source:
They found the page sifting a
little barley for his horse, and Sanchica cutting a rasher of bacon to be
paved with eggs for his dinner.

pairs.display: Meanwhile Murasaki went to her room an object of pity to anyone.
pairs.genji_half: Meanwhile Murasaki went to her room
pairs.quijote_half: an object of pity to anyone.
pairs.genji_source: Meanwhile Murasaki went to her room to get ready for the visit.
pairs.quijote_source: Adieu; God keep thee from being an object of pity to anyone.

pairs.display: With Murasaki the Crown Princess was quite as much at home as with her own mother, and has gone to law with him on the score of having given her promise of marriage.
pairs.genji_half: With Murasaki the Crown Princess was quite as much at home as with her own mother,
pairs.quijote_half: and has gone to law with him on the score of having given her promise of marriage.
pairs.genji_source:
With Murasaki the Crown Princess was quite as much at home as
with her own mother, and this part of the visit naturally passed off
agreeably enough.
pairs.quijote_source:
Minguilla, Mingo Silvato’s granddaughter, found it
out, and has gone to law with him on the score of having given her
promise of marriage.

pairs.display: Here she was visited by Murasaki, who found her sitting up in her white jacket, and handmaidens the impudent and witty Altisidora lifted up her voice and said in pathetic tones:
pairs.genji_half: Here she was visited by Murasaki, who found her sitting up in her white jacket,
pairs.quijote_half: and handmaidens the impudent and witty Altisidora lifted up her voice and said in pathetic tones:
pairs.genji_source:
Here she was
visited by Murasaki, who found her sitting up in her white jacket,
holding the little boy in her arms.
pairs.quijote_source:
While all were, as has been said,
observing him, suddenly from among the duennas and handmaidens the
impudent and witty Altisidora lifted up her voice and said in pathetic
tones:

pairs.display: It was a pretty sight; but it was hard indeed where’s the need Of spurring the flanks Of that ill-broken steed?
pairs.genji_half: It was a pretty sight; but it was hard indeed
pairs.quijote_half: where’s the need Of spurring the flanks Of that ill-broken steed?
pairs.genji_source:
It was a pretty sight; but it was
hard indeed for Murasaki to accustom herself to the idea that the
little Princess from Akashi was a mother!
pairs.quijote_source:
Give ear, cruel knight;
  Draw rein; where’s the need
Of spurring the flanks
  Of that ill-broken steed?

pairs.display: Presently, however, there came a note from the Crown Princess, of Saint Martin on his horse, dividing his cloak with the beggar.
pairs.genji_half: Presently, however, there came a note from the Crown Princess,
pairs.quijote_half: of Saint Martin on his horse, dividing his cloak with the beggar.
pairs.genji_source:
Presently, however, there came a note
from the Crown Princess, and in the answer that she dictated Murasaki
mentioned that she was unwell.
pairs.quijote_source:
The man uncovered it, and it was seen to be that of Saint Martin on his
horse, dividing his cloak with the beggar.

pairs.display: Suddenly a messenger arrived announcing that with all the details that are usually given in representations of his conversion.
pairs.genji_half: Suddenly a messenger arrived announcing that
pairs.quijote_half: with all the details that are usually given in representations of his conversion.
pairs.genji_source:
Suddenly a messenger arrived announcing that Murasaki appeared
to be sinking fast.
pairs.quijote_source:
They then raised another cloth which it appeared covered Saint Paul
falling from his horse, with all the details that are usually given in
representations of his conversion.

pairs.display: He readmitted them, had the boy-medium placed in a sealed room, trifle with what it may please heaven to do.
pairs.genji_half: He readmitted them, had the boy-medium placed in a sealed room,
pairs.quijote_half: trifle with what it may please heaven to do.
pairs.genji_source:
He
readmitted them, had the boy-medium placed in a sealed room, and
Murasaki carried secretly to a different part of the house.
pairs.quijote_source:
The wise man and the Christian
should not trifle with what it may please heaven to do.

pairs.display: But he remembered the occasion perfectly well; it was the night before Murasaki’s illness began, and as good fare as they could find in Saragossa.
pairs.genji_half: But he remembered the occasion perfectly well; it was the night before Murasaki’s illness began,
pairs.quijote_half: and as good fare as they could find in Saragossa.
pairs.genji_source:
But he remembered the occasion perfectly
well; it was the night before Murasaki’s illness began, and really he
had not said anything very bad....
pairs.quijote_source:
He said yes, with as much comfort
and as good fare as they could find in Saragossa.

pairs.display: But sooner or later the girl’s father would see to take part in the harness jousts which were held in that city every year.
pairs.genji_half: But sooner or later the girl’s father would see
pairs.quijote_half: to take part in the harness jousts which were held in that city every year.
pairs.genji_source:
But sooner or later the girl’s
father would see that Murasaki’s illness was quite unconnected with
the present estrangement.
pairs.quijote_source:
He replied, to
Saragossa, to take part in the harness jousts which were held in that
city every year.

pairs.display: He was Murasaki’s great favourite, for his life had come to an end.
pairs.genji_half: He was Murasaki’s great favourite,
pairs.quijote_half: for his life had come to an end.
pairs.genji_source:
He was Murasaki’s great favourite, and perhaps the
prettiest of all the children in the palace.
pairs.quijote_source:
Claudia recovered from her fainting fit, but not so
Don Vicente from the paroxysm that had overtaken him, for his life had
come to an end.

pairs.display: Though Murasaki was apparently no longer in immediate danger, unused as they were to shed them on any occasion.
pairs.genji_half: Though Murasaki was apparently no longer in immediate danger,
pairs.quijote_half: unused as they were to shed them on any occasion.
pairs.genji_source:
Though Murasaki was apparently no longer in immediate danger, her
illness had now lasted so many months that there appeared to be
little hope of recovery.
pairs.quijote_source:
So vehement and so piteous were the lamentations of Claudia that they
drew tears from Roque’s eyes, unused as they were to shed them on any
occasion.

pairs.display: The Woodman’s Song, resounding from so many lips, moved Murasaki intensely, his body to his father’s village, which was close by, for burial.
pairs.genji_half: The Woodman’s Song, resounding from so many lips, moved Murasaki intensely,
pairs.quijote_half: his body to his father’s village, which was close by, for burial.
pairs.genji_source:
The Woodman’s Song, resounding from so many
lips, moved Murasaki intensely, as indeed it would have done at any
time in her life.
pairs.quijote_source:
In
the end Roque Guinart directed Don Vicente’s servants to carry his body
to his father’s village, which was close by, for burial.

pairs.display: The procedure was the same as that and vanquished maintaining profound silence, waiting for the great Roque Guinart to speak.
pairs.genji_half: The procedure was the same as that
pairs.quijote_half: and vanquished maintaining profound silence, waiting for the great Roque Guinart to speak.
pairs.genji_source:
The procedure was the same as
that which Murasaki had witnessed many times before.
pairs.quijote_source:
The
squires made a ring round them, both victors and vanquished maintaining
profound silence, waiting for the great Roque Guinart to speak.

pairs.display: During the conversation that followed, and they repaired to the chamber where the rest of the gentlemen were assembled.
pairs.genji_half: During the conversation that followed,
pairs.quijote_half: and they repaired to the chamber where the rest of the gentlemen were assembled.
pairs.genji_source:
During the conversation that
followed, Murasaki made no allusion to her own approaching death.
pairs.quijote_source:
They then
quitted the room, Don Antonio locked the door, and they repaired to the
chamber where the rest of the gentlemen were assembled.

pairs.display: A cold wind had sprung up towards evening; but Murasaki, except Don Antonio’s two friends who were in the secret.
pairs.genji_half: A cold wind had sprung up towards evening; but Murasaki,
pairs.quijote_half: except Don Antonio’s two friends who were in the secret.
pairs.genji_source:
A cold wind had sprung up towards evening; but Murasaki, wanting to
get a better view of the garden, had been helped on to a couch by the
window.
pairs.quijote_source:
The questions and answers came to an end here, but not the wonder with
which all were filled, except Don Antonio’s two friends who were in the
secret.

pairs.display: It was many years since his thoughts about Murasaki had and Turks, who merely serve as rowers.
pairs.genji_half: It was many years since his thoughts about Murasaki had
pairs.quijote_half: and Turks, who merely serve as rowers.
pairs.genji_source:
It was many years since his thoughts about Murasaki had been
other than he could publish to all the world.
pairs.quijote_source:
The
rest of the crew of the brigantine are Moors and Turks, who merely serve
as rowers.

pairs.display: It frequently happens that those who by favour have risen to such an eminence and untied the cord that bound the hands of the Moorish girl.
pairs.genji_half: It frequently happens that those who by favour have risen to such an eminence
pairs.quijote_half: and untied the cord that bound the hands of the Moorish girl.
pairs.genji_source:
It frequently happens that those who by favour have risen to such an
eminence as that which Murasaki enjoyed are subjected to a good deal
of general spite.
pairs.quijote_source:
The viceroy, touched with compassion, went up to her
without speaking and untied the cord that bound the hands of the Moorish
girl.

pairs.display: Those of Murasaki’s ladies who had been long in her service were still dressed in deep mourning for her own beauty as for my wealth.
pairs.genji_half: Those of Murasaki’s ladies who had been long in her service were still dressed in deep mourning
pairs.quijote_half: for her own beauty as for my wealth.
pairs.genji_source:
Those of Murasaki’s ladies who had been long in her service were still
dressed in deep mourning and were inconsolable as ever for her loss.
pairs.quijote_source:
She is Ana Felix, surnamed Ricote, celebrated as much for her own beauty
as for my wealth.

pairs.display: Once he sent for Tō no Chūjō’s brother Kurōdo no Ben for rescuing Don Gaspar Gregorio from the danger in which he had been left.
pairs.genji_half: Once he sent for Tō no Chūjō’s brother Kurōdo no Ben
pairs.quijote_half: for rescuing Don Gaspar Gregorio from the danger in which he had been left.
pairs.genji_source:
Once he sent for Tō no Chūjō’s
brother Kurōdo no Ben and gave him a formal message for the Emperor.
pairs.quijote_source:
They
then tried to devise some scheme for rescuing Don Gaspar Gregorio from
the danger in which he had been left.

pairs.display: They had still no proof that it was Genji who was her lover, was extremely happy to see Ana Felix in her house.
pairs.genji_half: They had still no proof that it was Genji who was her lover,
pairs.quijote_half: was extremely happy to see Ana Felix in her house.
pairs.genji_source:
They had
still no proof that it was Genji who was her lover, but several of
them thought that they had recognized him and his name was whispered
among them.
pairs.quijote_source:
The wife of Don Antonio Moreno, so the history says, was extremely happy
to see Ana Felix in her house.

pairs.display: I am very sorry indeed that you have come so far,’ nor whether the defiance was in joke or in earnest.
pairs.genji_half: I am very sorry indeed that you have come so far,’
pairs.quijote_half: nor whether the defiance was in joke or in earnest.
pairs.genji_source:
I am very sorry
indeed that you have come so far,’ and pretending to be very much
upset, he looked at Genji, laughing.
pairs.quijote_source:
Don Antonio replied that he
neither knew who he was nor whether the defiance was in joke or in
earnest.

pairs.display: He had invited Genji when he had met him at the Palace only a few days before and by some means or other find out who he was.
pairs.genji_half: He had invited Genji when he had met him at the Palace only a few days before
pairs.quijote_half: and by some means or other find out who he was.
pairs.genji_source:
He
had invited Genji when he had met him at the Palace only a few days
before and was extremely annoyed when he did not appear.
pairs.quijote_source:
The viceroy bade Don
Antonio hasten after him, and by some means or other find out who he was.

pairs.display: Such an offer was perhaps somewhat forward, and Ricote in the viceroy’s house.
pairs.genji_half: Such an offer was perhaps somewhat forward,
pairs.quijote_half: and Ricote in the viceroy’s house.
pairs.genji_source:
Such an offer was perhaps somewhat forward, but the place she
had indicated was such a good one that Genji at once accepted the
invitation.
pairs.quijote_source:
Ana Felix remained with Don Antonio’s wife, and Ricote in
the viceroy’s house.

pairs.display: This made Genji’s visits particularly imprudent, at the parting between Don Gregorio and Ana Felix.
pairs.genji_half: This made Genji’s visits particularly imprudent,
pairs.quijote_half: at the parting between Don Gregorio and Ana Felix.
pairs.genji_source:
This made Genji’s visits
particularly imprudent, but it was just this added risk which attracted
him and induced him to repeat them.
pairs.quijote_source:
There were tears and sighs, swoonings and sobs, at the
parting between Don Gregorio and Ana Felix.

pairs.display: Genji was indeed equipped more like a traveller setting out as well by the al at the beginning as by the i they end with.
pairs.genji_half: Genji was indeed equipped more like a traveller setting out
pairs.quijote_half: as well by the al at the beginning as by the i they end with.
pairs.genji_source:
Genji was indeed equipped more like a traveller setting
out from the Capital than like one returning from the provinces.
pairs.quijote_source:
Alheli and alfaqui are seen to be
Arabic, as well by the al at the beginning as by the i they end with.

pairs.display: The funeral was carried out with the utmost pomp, us our dinner to the pasture.
pairs.genji_half: The funeral was carried out with the utmost pomp,
pairs.quijote_half: us our dinner to the pasture.
pairs.genji_source:
The
funeral was carried out with the utmost pomp, the bier being attended
not only by her servants, but by all Genji’s servants and retainers.
pairs.quijote_source:
My daughter Sanchica will
bring us our dinner to the pasture.

pairs.display: He was just setting out to report this to Genji to do with the Quixotes?
pairs.genji_half: He was just setting out to report this to Genji
pairs.quijote_half: to do with the Quixotes?
pairs.genji_source:
He was just setting out to
report this to Genji when the moon came out from behind a cloud,
lighting up the front of the house.
pairs.quijote_source:
But what
have the Panzas to do with the Quixotes?

pairs.display: But this year there were none of the usual religious festivals and hide itself under Dapple.
pairs.genji_half: But this year there were none of the usual religious festivals
pairs.quijote_half: and hide itself under Dapple.
pairs.genji_source:
But
this year there were none of the usual religious festivals and
processions to distract him, and Genji became more and more
restless.
pairs.quijote_source:
In its terror it ran to take shelter and hide itself under
Dapple.

pairs.display: How exciting it would be to live with a father, who, the oddest transformation and decoration that ever ass in the world underwent.
pairs.genji_half: How exciting it would be to live with a father, who,
pairs.quijote_half: the oddest transformation and decoration that ever ass in the world underwent.
pairs.genji_source:
How
exciting it would be to live with a father, who, according to Genji’s
own showing, played far, far better even than this!
pairs.quijote_source:
He had also fixed
the mitre on Dapple’s head, the oddest transformation and decoration that
ever ass in the world underwent.

pairs.display: Formerly they had both been in Genji’s personal service; but for a long my lord the king, Was meant for me alone.
pairs.genji_half: Formerly they had both been in Genji’s personal service; but for a long
pairs.quijote_half: my lord the king, Was meant for me alone.
pairs.genji_source:
Formerly they had both been
in Genji’s personal service; but for a long while past they had waited
upon Murasaki, and were both devotedly attached to her.
pairs.quijote_source:
Adventure it let none,
For this emprise, my lord the king,
  Was meant for me alone.

pairs.display: Their carriages remained side by side, but Genji did not even draw up the side-curtains, for only eight months of service thou hast given me the best island the sea girds or surrounds!
pairs.genji_half: Their carriages remained side by side, but Genji did not even draw up the side-curtains,
pairs.quijote_half: for only eight months of service thou hast given me the best island the sea girds or surrounds!
pairs.genji_source:
Their carriages remained side by side, but Genji did not even draw up
the side-curtains, which was a disappointment to more persons than
one.
pairs.quijote_source:
Oh thou, generous
above all the Alexanders, since for only eight months of service thou
hast given me the best island the sea girds or surrounds!

pairs.display: It was not written with very great display of penmanship, when my tongue Is cold in death, believe me, unto thee My voice shall raise its tributary song.
pairs.genji_half: It was not written with very great display of penmanship,
pairs.quijote_half: when my tongue Is cold in death, believe me, unto thee My voice shall raise its tributary song.
pairs.genji_source:
It was not written with very great display of penmanship, but there was
(or Genji fancied that there was) a peculiar distinction and refinement
in the writing.
pairs.quijote_source:
But not in life alone, methinks, to me
    Belongs the office; Lady, when my tongue
Is cold in death, believe me, unto thee
    My voice shall raise its tributary song.

pairs.display: But Genji had a peculiar gift of sympathy, Oh, Jealousy! put chains upon these hands, And bind me with thy strongest cord, Disdain.
pairs.genji_half: But Genji had a peculiar gift of sympathy,
pairs.quijote_half: Oh, Jealousy! put chains upon these hands, And bind me with thy strongest cord, Disdain.
pairs.genji_source:
But Genji had a peculiar gift of
sympathy, which enabled him to penetrate the most obstinate gloom, the
most imperturbable gravity.
pairs.quijote_source:
Oh, thou fierce tyrant of the realms of love,
Oh, Jealousy! put chains upon these hands,
And bind me with thy strongest cord, Disdain.

pairs.display: The longer Suzaku reflected, inventor of music; thou that always risest and, notwithstanding appearances, never settest!
pairs.genji_half: The longer Suzaku reflected,
pairs.quijote_half: inventor of music; thou that always risest and, notwithstanding appearances, never settest!
pairs.genji_source:
The longer Suzaku reflected, the more difficult did it appear to
find any one more promising than Genji.

pairs.display: But she did not show, or hoped she did not show, any of these feelings, and the next day reached the inn where the mishap of the blanket had befallen him.
pairs.genji_half: But she did not show, or hoped she did not show, any of these feelings,
pairs.quijote_half: and the next day reached the inn where the mishap of the blanket had befallen him.
pairs.genji_source:
But she did not show, or hoped she did not show, any of these feelings,
and in the preparations for Nyosan’s arrival she gave Genji all the
help she could.
pairs.quijote_source:
As for him, coming out upon the high road, he made for El Toboso, and the
next day reached the inn where the mishap of the blanket had befallen
him.

pairs.display: Here too were shown the horses from the Royal Stables on the bleeding breast of Don Vicente, whom a death spasm seized the same instant.
pairs.genji_half: Here too were shown the horses from the Royal Stables
pairs.quijote_half: on the bleeding breast of Don Vicente, whom a death spasm seized the same instant.
pairs.genji_source:
Here too were shown the horses from the Royal Stables
and the hawks from the Royal Falconry, that had been decreed as
presents for Genji.
pairs.quijote_source:
Claudia wrung his hands, and her own heart was so wrung that she lay
fainting on the bleeding breast of Don Vicente, whom a death spasm seized
the same instant.

pairs.display: The day was now very hot, scorning slumber and the lazy down, Adopt the toilsome life of bloodstained arms!
pairs.genji_half: The day was now very hot,
pairs.quijote_half: scorning slumber and the lazy down, Adopt the toilsome life of bloodstained arms!
pairs.genji_source:
The
day was now very hot, but they thought that flushed cheeks became
Prince Genji very well.
pairs.quijote_source:
O shining light,
O beacon, polestar, path and guide of all
Who, scorning slumber and the lazy down,
Adopt the toilsome life of bloodstained arms!

pairs.display: The house was quite near the highway and Genji, alighting That thy heart is brass in hardness, And thy snowy bosom stone.
pairs.genji_half: The house was quite near the highway and Genji, alighting
pairs.quijote_half: That thy heart is brass in hardness, And thy snowy bosom stone.
pairs.genji_source:
The house was quite near the highway
and Genji, alighting for a moment from the carriage, stood near the
gate to listen.
pairs.quijote_source:
True it is, Olalla, sometimes
  Thou hast all too plainly shown
That thy heart is brass in hardness,
  And thy snowy bosom stone.

pairs.display: It was not as though Genji had been a stranger (though even who knew all about them, and strangled him in his arms at Roncesvalles.
pairs.genji_half: It was not as though Genji had been a stranger (though even
pairs.quijote_half: who knew all about them, and strangled him in his arms at Roncesvalles.
pairs.genji_source:
It was not as though Genji had been a stranger
(though even then it would have been bad enough, considering his rank
and influence).
pairs.quijote_source:
Though cunning devices did
not avail him against Bernardo del Carpio, who knew all about them, and
strangled him in his arms at Roncesvalles.

pairs.display: Fujitsubo was all this while extremely exercised in mind concerning the future of her niece, that Corchuelo delivered were past counting, and came thicker than hops or hail.
pairs.genji_half: Fujitsubo was all this while extremely exercised in mind concerning the future of her niece,
pairs.quijote_half: that Corchuelo delivered were past counting, and came thicker than hops or hail.
pairs.genji_source:
Fujitsubo was all this while extremely exercised in mind concerning
the future of her niece, the youngest daughter of Prince Hyōbukyō, for
Genji’s estrangement from the father seemed to block every avenue of
advancement.
pairs.quijote_source:
The cuts, thrusts, down
strokes, back strokes and doubles, that Corchuelo delivered were past
counting, and came thicker than hops or hail.

pairs.display: Even Higekuro’s personal servants, the maids Moku no Kimi and Chūjō no Omoto, Sunrise loves, with sunset ended, When the guest hath gone his way.
pairs.genji_half: Even Higekuro’s personal servants, the maids Moku no Kimi and Chūjō no Omoto,
pairs.quijote_half: Sunrise loves, with sunset ended, When the guest hath gone his way.
pairs.genji_source:
Even Higekuro’s personal servants, the maids
Moku no Kimi and Chūjō no Omoto, were against him in this matter, and
expressed loud indignation at the prospect of Tamakatsura’s arrival.
pairs.quijote_source:
There are passions, transient, fleeting,
  Loves in hostelries declar’d,
Sunrise loves, with sunset ended,
  When the guest hath gone his way.

pairs.display: Though it was very And thy fickle fits between, Hope is there--at least the border Of her garment may be seen.
pairs.genji_half: Though it was very
pairs.quijote_half: And thy fickle fits between, Hope is there--at least the border Of her garment may be seen.
pairs.genji_source:
Though it
was very dark she recognized Genji’s figure.
pairs.quijote_source:
Yet for all that, in thy coyness,
  And thy fickle fits between,
Hope is there--at least the border
  Of her garment may be seen.

pairs.display: Though so quickly improvised, their quarters were admirably clean and well-provided, as they wanted to halt and drink at a little spring there was there.
pairs.genji_half: Though so quickly improvised, their quarters were admirably clean and well-provided,
pairs.quijote_half: as they wanted to halt and drink at a little spring there was there.
pairs.genji_source:
Though so quickly
improvised, their quarters were admirably clean and well-provided, for
the steward’s son had previously been a trusted house-servant of
Genji’s and had also worked at the Great Hall.
pairs.quijote_source:
Master Nicholas here called out to them to wait a while, as they wanted
to halt and drink at a little spring there was there.

pairs.display: Genji too roused himself at last he paid love’s bitter penalty, And left the marble to preserve his name.
pairs.genji_half: Genji too roused himself at last
pairs.quijote_half: he paid love’s bitter penalty, And left the marble to preserve his name.
pairs.genji_source:
Genji too roused
himself at last and forced himself to pray inwardly to the Buddha.
pairs.quijote_source:
Nor youth nor beauty saved her from the claim
Of death; he paid love’s bitter penalty,
  And left the marble to preserve his name.

pairs.display: There were however one or two younger maids who had heard a great deal about this Prince Genji and the two nieces to the knights of a very holy order called the Order of St. John.
pairs.genji_half: There were however one or two younger maids who had heard a great deal about this Prince Genji
pairs.quijote_half: and the two nieces to the knights of a very holy order called the Order of St. John.
pairs.genji_source:
There were however one or
two younger maids who had heard a great deal about this Prince Genji
and were ready to fall in love with him at a moment’s notice.
pairs.quijote_source:
The seven
daughters belong to the kings of Spain and the two nieces to the knights
of a very holy order called the Order of St. John.

pairs.display: Jijū and the other ladies-in-waiting had heard so much about Genji who came here to paint anything that might turn up.
pairs.genji_half: Jijū and the other ladies-in-waiting had heard so much about Genji
pairs.quijote_half: who came here to paint anything that might turn up.
pairs.genji_source:
Jijū and the other
ladies-in-waiting had heard so much about Genji and were so anxious to
catch sight of him that they were more than ready to forgive his
uncivil intrusion.
pairs.quijote_source:
Here is the
news of the village; La Berrueca has married her daughter to a
good-for-nothing painter, who came here to paint anything that might turn
up.

pairs.display: Genji was so busy that he had barely time to pay an occasional surreptitious visit even to his dearest friends, although of the giant breed which is always arrogant and ill-conditioned, he alone was affable and well-bred.
pairs.genji_half: Genji was so busy that he had barely time to pay an occasional surreptitious visit even to his dearest friends,
pairs.quijote_half: although of the giant breed which is always arrogant and ill-conditioned, he alone was affable and well-bred.
pairs.genji_source:
Genji was
so busy that he had barely time to pay an occasional surreptitious
visit even to his dearest friends, and the autumn passed without his
returning to the Hitachi Palace.
pairs.quijote_source:
He approved highly of the
giant Morgante, because, although of the giant breed which is always
arrogant and ill-conditioned, he alone was affable and well-bred.

pairs.display: Fujitsubo now retired to her own house and Genji, waiting about for a chance of visiting her, and they gave the Judge an account of his extraordinary humour, with which he was not a little amused.
pairs.genji_half: Fujitsubo now retired to her own house and Genji, waiting about for a chance of visiting her,
pairs.quijote_half: and they gave the Judge an account of his extraordinary humour, with which he was not a little amused.
pairs.genji_source:
Fujitsubo now retired to her own house and Genji, waiting about for a
chance of visiting her, was seldom at the Great Hall and was
consequently in very ill odour there.
pairs.quijote_source:
Those who understood him
returned him thanks for this service, and they gave the Judge an account
of his extraordinary humour, with which he was not a little amused.

pairs.display: He pulled straight the back of Genji’s robe which had become a little crumpled, and there is some difference between Peter and Peter.
pairs.genji_half: He pulled straight the back of Genji’s robe which had become a little crumpled,
pairs.quijote_half: and there is some difference between Peter and Peter.
pairs.genji_source:
He pulled straight the back
of Genji’s robe which had become a little crumpled, and indeed short
of bringing him his shoes performed in the friendliest way every
possible small service.
pairs.quijote_source:
Mind how you talk, master barber; for shaving is
not everything, and there is some difference between Peter and Peter.

pairs.display: But he was now in full view and Genji realized in a moment that Chūjō and Durandarte, and all who were there, were to be disenchanted.
pairs.genji_half: But he was now in full view and Genji realized in a moment that Chūjō
pairs.quijote_half: and Durandarte, and all who were there, were to be disenchanted.
pairs.genji_source:
But he was now in full
view and Genji realized in a moment that Chūjō had all the while known
who he was and had been amusing himself at his expense.
pairs.quijote_source:
He told me, moreover, that in course of time he
would let me know how he and Belerma, and Durandarte, and all who were
there, were to be disenchanted.

pairs.display: For years they had been in open rivalry for Genji’s favours, and the whole place seemed a field of sorrow and an abode of misfortune.
pairs.genji_half: For years they had been in open rivalry for Genji’s favours,
pairs.quijote_half: and the whole place seemed a field of sorrow and an abode of misfortune.
pairs.genji_source:
For years they had been in open rivalry for Genji’s
favours, but even after that wretched affair of the coaches (though it
must be admitted that this had greatly incensed her)
pairs.quijote_source:
The servants wept, Claudia swooned away again and again, and
the whole place seemed a field of sorrow and an abode of misfortune.

pairs.display: Now in a moment all this was undone, and when she heard Genji himself entreating My teeth like topazes exalt My beauty to the sky.
pairs.genji_half: Now in a moment all this was undone, and when she heard Genji himself entreating
pairs.quijote_half: My teeth like topazes exalt My beauty to the sky.
pairs.genji_source:
Now in a moment all this was
undone, and when she heard Genji himself entreating her to abandon the
journey her heart beat violently, and the wildest thoughts agitated
her brain.
pairs.quijote_source:
And though my nose be rather flat,
    And though my mouth be wide,
My teeth like topazes exalt
    My beauty to the sky.

pairs.display: What (Genji asked himself) A crazy man his life he passed, But in his senses died at last.
pairs.genji_half: What (Genji asked himself)
pairs.quijote_half: A crazy man his life he passed, But in his senses died at last.
pairs.genji_source: What (Genji asked himself) must they think of him?

pairs.display: In the Capital Genji’s Have thou no fear If at each step thou findest death is near.
pairs.genji_half: In the Capital Genji’s
pairs.quijote_half: Have thou no fear If at each step thou findest death is near.
pairs.genji_source: In the Capital Genji’s absence was still universally deplored.
pairs.quijote_source:
Heedless of hindrance or impediment,
  Have thou no fear
If at each step thou findest death is near.

pairs.display: So he announced himself; but the journey had reduced him to such a plight that or at least was not two hours since, Don Vicente Torrellas.
pairs.genji_half: So he announced himself; but the journey had reduced him to such a plight that
pairs.quijote_half: or at least was not two hours since, Don Vicente Torrellas.
pairs.genji_source:
So he announced himself; but the journey had reduced him to
such a plight that Genji would scarce have known that this tattered,
dripping mass was a human being at all.
pairs.quijote_source:
Thou knowest that this
Torrellas has a son who is called, or at least was not two hours since,
Don Vicente Torrellas.

pairs.display: So well had this been recognized by his father, the old Emperor, and the beauty of Ana Felix were the admiration of all who were present.
pairs.genji_half: So well had this been recognized by his father, the old Emperor,
pairs.quijote_half: and the beauty of Ana Felix were the admiration of all who were present.
pairs.genji_source:
So well had this been recognized by
his father, the old Emperor, that although Genji was his favourite son
he had given special instructions that he was to remain a commoner.
pairs.quijote_source:
Seen side by
side, the comeliness of Don Gregorio and the beauty of Ana Felix were the
admiration of all who were present.

pairs.display: To Genji these outbursts and, as best thou canst, say to them: Hold off!
pairs.genji_half: To Genji these outbursts
pairs.quijote_half: and, as best thou canst, say to them: Hold off!
pairs.genji_source:
To Genji these outbursts were by no means
unattractive.
pairs.quijote_source:
But ere they touch thee warn them, and, as best thou canst, say to them:

Hold off!

pairs.display: The nurse was allowed to read Genji’s letters, and though she did so with passionate interest, and one of the guards said he was a great talker and a very elegant Latin scholar.
pairs.genji_half: The nurse was allowed to read Genji’s letters, and though she did so with passionate interest,
pairs.quijote_half: and one of the guards said he was a great talker and a very elegant Latin scholar.
pairs.genji_source:
The nurse was allowed to read Genji’s
letters, and though she did so with passionate interest, she could not
but feel somewhat jealous of her mistress’s strange and unforeseen good
fortune.
pairs.quijote_source:
This one was in the dress of a student, and one of the guards said he was
a great talker and a very elegant Latin scholar.

pairs.display: When the message reached Genji it was already growing dark; the tide was full, what tabors, timbrels, and rebecks!
pairs.genji_half: When the message reached Genji it was already growing dark; the tide was full,
pairs.quijote_half: what tabors, timbrels, and rebecks!
pairs.genji_source:
When the message
reached Genji it was already growing dark; the tide was full, and the
cranes along the river-mouth had with one accord set up their strange
and moving cry.
pairs.quijote_source:
What hautboys and Zamora bagpipes we shall hear, what tabors,
timbrels, and rebecks!

pairs.display: Genji’s private plan of affiancing her to the boy-Emperor would, of this bosom’s depths of bitterness, To ease my heart and plant a sting in thine.
pairs.genji_half: Genji’s private plan of affiancing her to the boy-Emperor would,
pairs.quijote_half: of this bosom’s depths of bitterness, To ease my heart and plant a sting in thine.
pairs.genji_source:
Genji’s private plan of affiancing her to the boy-Emperor
would, now that Suzaku had displayed so marked an inclination towards
her, be difficult to pursue without too deeply offending his
brother.
pairs.quijote_source:
Then listen, not to dulcet harmony,
But to a discord wrung by mad despair
Out of this bosom’s depths of bitterness,
To ease my heart and plant a sting in thine.

pairs.display: Tō no Chūjō’s daughter, as the grandchild of the Senior Minister, And deadly is the force of jealousy; Long absence makes of life a dreary void;
pairs.genji_half: Tō no Chūjō’s daughter, as the grandchild of the Senior Minister,
pairs.quijote_half: And deadly is the force of jealousy; Long absence makes of life a dreary void;
pairs.genji_source:
Tō no Chūjō’s daughter, as the grandchild of the Senior
Minister, was treated on all sides with the utmost deference and
consideration, and she had now become the Emperor’s favourite playmate.

pairs.display: On the eve of the festival-day Genji was to undergo the ritual of Purification flat poll, and snub nose, blind of one eye and not very sound in the other.
pairs.genji_half: On the eve of the festival-day Genji was to undergo the ritual of Purification
pairs.quijote_half: flat poll, and snub nose, blind of one eye and not very sound in the other.
pairs.genji_source:
On the eve of the festival-day
Genji was to undergo the ritual of Purification and the presents which
are customary in connexion with this occasion began pouring in thick
and fast.
pairs.quijote_source:
There was besides in the inn, as servant, an
Asturian lass with a broad face, flat poll, and snub nose, blind of one
eye and not very sound in the other.

pairs.display: Concerning the Emperor, too, Genji’s conscience was very uneasy; for had Ryōzen indeed discovered the true story of his birth, in anguish of suspense? Absence.
pairs.genji_half: Concerning the Emperor, too, Genji’s conscience was very uneasy; for had Ryōzen indeed discovered the true story of his birth,
pairs.quijote_half: in anguish of suspense? Absence.
pairs.genji_source:
Concerning the Emperor, too, Genji’s conscience was very uneasy; for
had Ryōzen indeed discovered the true story of his birth, he must now
be living in a state of continual apprehension.
pairs.quijote_source:
What holds my heart in anguish of suspense?
    Absence.

pairs.display: Meanwhile Genji was inspecting but what wonder if the friendship of Lothario could not stand firm?
pairs.genji_half: Meanwhile Genji was inspecting
pairs.quijote_half: but what wonder if the friendship of Lothario could not stand firm?
pairs.genji_source:
Meanwhile Genji was inspecting
the little train-bearers and pages.
pairs.quijote_source:
Camilla yielded, Camilla fell; but what wonder if the friendship of
Lothario could not stand firm?

pairs.display: The Chinese zithern was and landing forces fortified the mouth of the harbour and waited quietly until Don John retired.
pairs.genji_half: The Chinese zithern was
pairs.quijote_half: and landing forces fortified the mouth of the harbour and waited quietly until Don John retired.
pairs.genji_source:
The Chinese zithern was as usual played by
Genji.
pairs.quijote_source:
As it was, El
Uchali took refuge at Modon, which is an island near Navarino, and
landing forces fortified the mouth of the harbour and waited quietly
until Don John retired.

pairs.display: But all the same Prince Genji often sends for me to wait upon him the high-bred air, the brilliant attire of my beloved Zoraida as she presented herself before my eyes.
pairs.genji_half: But all the same Prince Genji often sends for me to wait upon him
pairs.quijote_half: the high-bred air, the brilliant attire of my beloved Zoraida as she presented herself before my eyes.
pairs.genji_source:
But all the
same Prince Genji often sends for me to wait upon him and likes me
sometimes to tell him about anything interesting that I have seen or
heard.
pairs.quijote_source:
It would be
beyond my power now to describe to you the great beauty, the high-bred
air, the brilliant attire of my beloved Zoraida as she presented herself
before my eyes.

pairs.display: It may be imagined then what an enchantment these first spring days, everywhere so delightful, and more valiant than the famous Amadis of Gaul?
pairs.genji_half: It may be imagined then what an enchantment these first spring days, everywhere so delightful,
pairs.quijote_half: and more valiant than the famous Amadis of Gaul?
pairs.genji_source:
It may be imagined then
what an enchantment these first spring days, everywhere so delightful,
cast upon the gardens of Genji’s palace, with their paths of jade-dust,
their groves and lakes.
pairs.quijote_source:
For tell me, who was more
virtuous and more valiant than the famous Amadis of Gaul?

pairs.display: It would be impossible here to describe in any way that would not be both tedious if his uncle the abbot has a mind to have him trained for the Church.
pairs.genji_half: It would be impossible here to describe in any way that would not be both tedious
pairs.quijote_half: if his uncle the abbot has a mind to have him trained for the Church.
pairs.genji_source:
It would be impossible here to describe in
any way that would not be both tedious and inadequate the beauties
of the four domains which Genji had allotted to his favourites.
pairs.quijote_source:
Remember that
Sanchico is now full fifteen, and it is right he should go to school, if
his uncle the abbot has a mind to have him trained for the Church.

pairs.display: But the principal suitor by Love itself be told.
pairs.genji_half: But the principal suitor
pairs.quijote_half: by Love itself be told.
pairs.genji_source: But the principal suitor was Genji’s half-brother Prince Sochi
pairs.quijote_source: Then shall my tale by Love itself be told.

pairs.display: With these words Genji and in their eyes, and lightness in their feet, they looked the best dancers in the world.
pairs.genji_half: With these words Genji
pairs.quijote_half: and in their eyes, and lightness in their feet, they looked the best dancers in the world.
pairs.genji_source:
With these words Genji
re-appeared from behind the curtains.
pairs.quijote_source:
The notes of a Zamora
bagpipe accompanied them, and with modesty in their countenances and in
their eyes, and lightness in their feet, they looked the best dancers in
the world.

pairs.display: Nothing had been said about restoring her to her real father, The shades below acknowledge me.
pairs.genji_half: Nothing had been said about restoring her to her real father,
pairs.quijote_half: The shades below acknowledge me.
pairs.genji_source:
Nothing had been said about restoring her to her
real father, and Tō no Chūjō was certain that under the circumstances
Genji would make every possible effort to avoid doing so.
pairs.quijote_source:
The heavens above us own me; nay,
  The shades below acknowledge me.

pairs.display: But she constantly wondered whether Genji thought that she was really enjoying herself with this repulsive man, but only before he had become so, in France, and in the time of the aforesaid emperor Charlemagne.
pairs.genji_half: But she constantly wondered whether Genji thought that she was really enjoying herself with this repulsive man,
pairs.quijote_half: but only before he had become so, in France, and in the time of the aforesaid emperor Charlemagne.
pairs.genji_source:
But
she constantly wondered whether Genji thought that she was really
enjoying herself with this repulsive man, and blushed at the low
opinion he must now have formed of her taste.
pairs.quijote_source:
This phrase and
expression he could not have learned while he was enchanted, but only
before he had become so, in France, and in the time of the aforesaid
emperor Charlemagne.

pairs.display: The latter of course had not that complete self-possession and authority of manner which His or are these noble personages likely to let the beasts fare badly when they treat their owners in such elegant style?
pairs.genji_half: The latter of course had not that complete self-possession and authority of manner which His
pairs.quijote_half: or are these noble personages likely to let the beasts fare badly when they treat their owners in such elegant style?
pairs.genji_source:
The latter of course had not that complete self-possession
and authority of manner which His Majesty had naturally acquired
during his years of rule; but Yūgiri had distinctly the better
complexion.
pairs.quijote_source:
Was that a time to bethink thee of thy Dapple, or are these
noble personages likely to let the beasts fare badly when they treat
their owners in such elegant style?

pairs.display: Suzaku was extremely fond of this princess, Come Death, so subtly veiled
pairs.genji_half: Suzaku was extremely fond of this princess,
pairs.quijote_half: Come Death, so subtly veiled
pairs.genji_source:
Suzaku was extremely fond of
this princess, and their daughter, Princess Nyosan, was undoubtedly
his favourite child.
pairs.quijote_source:
Another time he sang:

  Come Death, so subtly veiled

pairs.display: A brilliant match for any other woman; but when it came to imagining him as Nyosan’s husband, and those that remained lay stretched on the ground as if in a swoon.
pairs.genji_half: A brilliant match for any other woman; but when it came to imagining him as Nyosan’s husband,
pairs.quijote_half: and those that remained lay stretched on the ground as if in a swoon.
pairs.genji_source:
A
brilliant match for any other woman; but when it came to imagining him
as Nyosan’s husband, Kashiwagi (as indeed every one else of whom Suzaku
thought) seemed somehow hopelessly inadequate.
pairs.quijote_source:
By this time the bearded band of duennas, the
Trifaldi and all, had vanished from the garden, and those that remained
lay stretched on the ground as if in a swoon.

pairs.display: Lady Wistaria, Nyosan’s mother, for thee; What fine long holland cloaks!
pairs.genji_half: Lady Wistaria, Nyosan’s mother,
pairs.quijote_half: for thee; What fine long holland cloaks!
pairs.genji_source:
Lady Wistaria, Nyosan’s
mother, was a sister of Fujitsubo.
pairs.quijote_source:
What damask breeches make for thee;
    What fine long holland cloaks!

pairs.display: Murasaki had already heard they were all good-looking young fellows, except Ricote, who was a man somewhat advanced in years.
pairs.genji_half: Murasaki had already heard
pairs.quijote_half: they were all good-looking young fellows, except Ricote, who was a man somewhat advanced in years.
pairs.genji_source: Murasaki had already heard some rumour of Suzaku’s intention.
pairs.quijote_source:
They threw down their staves, took off their pilgrim’s cloaks
and remained in their under-clothing; they were all good-looking young
fellows, except Ricote, who was a man somewhat advanced in years.

pairs.display: And though such nights as the one I am describing had become common enough since Nyosan’s arrival, Thy praises singing still shall hold its way, And make the waters of oblivion stay.
pairs.genji_half: And though such nights as the one I am describing had become common enough since Nyosan’s arrival,
pairs.quijote_half: Thy praises singing still shall hold its way, And make the waters of oblivion stay.
pairs.genji_source:
And though such nights as the one I am
describing had become common enough since Nyosan’s arrival, Murasaki
still felt awkward and lopsided as she tried to arrange herself in bed.
pairs.quijote_source:
My soul, from this strait prison-house set free,
    As o’er the Stygian lake it floats along,
Thy praises singing still shall hold its way,
And make the waters of oblivion stay.

pairs.display: Nyosan was still so young that she liked to have her nurses sleeping close at hand, and he found that together with those of the night before they made up three thousand and twenty-nine.
pairs.genji_half: Nyosan was still so young that she liked to have her nurses sleeping close at hand,
pairs.quijote_half: and he found that together with those of the night before they made up three thousand and twenty-nine.
pairs.genji_source:
Nyosan was still so young that she liked
to have her nurses sleeping close at hand, and when Genji opened the
passage-door, they sat up and craned their necks after him.
pairs.quijote_source:
The duped Don
Quixote did not miss a single stroke of the count, and he found that
together with those of the night before they made up three thousand and
twenty-nine.

pairs.display: She blushed, not however at the idea of Kashiwagi’s having seen her, Parading in thy shape, deceives the eye, And makes its vileness bright as virtue show.
pairs.genji_half: She blushed, not however at the idea of Kashiwagi’s having seen her,
pairs.quijote_half: Parading in thy shape, deceives the eye, And makes its vileness bright as virtue show.
pairs.genji_source:
She blushed,
not however at the idea of Kashiwagi’s having seen her, but at the
recollection that Genji had more than once warned her not to expose
herself in Yūgiri’s presence.
pairs.quijote_source:
Thy semblance, and upon it to bestow
  Thy veil, wherewith at times hypocrisy,
  Parading in thy shape, deceives the eye,
And makes its vileness bright as virtue show.

pairs.display: And even now, since it is clear that Genji does not take any interest in her, it was whispered that he was over-quarrelsome, and of his brother that he was lachrymose.
pairs.genji_half: And even now, since it is clear that Genji does not take any interest in her,
pairs.quijote_half: it was whispered that he was over-quarrelsome, and of his brother that he was lachrymose.
pairs.genji_source:
And even now, since it is clear
that Genji does not take any interest in her, I gather they are by no
means displeased that I should feel as I do.
pairs.quijote_source:
Of Don Galaor, the brother of Amadis of
Gaul, it was whispered that he was over-quarrelsome, and of his brother
that he was lachrymose.

pairs.display: He was really Genji’s son, one sepulchre, one memory, Slays, and entombs, and brings to life again.
pairs.genji_half: He was really Genji’s son,
pairs.quijote_half: one sepulchre, one memory, Slays, and entombs, and brings to life again.
pairs.genji_source: He was really Genji’s son, not the old Emperor’s.
pairs.quijote_source:
So runs the strange tale, how the lovers twain
One sword, one sepulchre, one memory,
  Slays, and entombs, and brings to life again.

pairs.display: But if she took orders Suzaku could establish her very comfortably in that roomy pleasant palace in the Third Ward, Thou scatterest wounds, but, ah, the balm To heal them dost withhold!
pairs.genji_half: But if she took orders Suzaku could establish her very comfortably in that roomy pleasant palace in the Third Ward,
pairs.quijote_half: Thou scatterest wounds, but, ah, the balm To heal them dost withhold!
pairs.genji_source:
But if she
took orders Suzaku could establish her very comfortably in that
roomy pleasant palace in the Third Ward, which his father, the
old Emperor, had once presented to him.
pairs.quijote_source:
Adventures seeking thou dost rove,
    To others bringing woe;
Thou scatterest wounds, but, ah, the balm
    To heal them dost withhold!

pairs.display: While he was alive, he could keep an eye upon her himself; and Genji, Passes, leaves no trace behind it, Leaves no image on the soul.
pairs.genji_half: While he was alive, he could keep an eye upon her himself; and Genji,
pairs.quijote_half: Passes, leaves no trace behind it, Leaves no image on the soul.
pairs.genji_source:
While he was alive, he could
keep an eye upon her himself; and Genji, whatever might be his other
preoccupations, would surely not be so unfeeling as wholly to abandon
her.
pairs.quijote_source:
Love that springs up swift and sudden,
  Here to-day, to-morrow flown,
Passes, leaves no trace behind it,
  Leaves no image on the soul.

pairs.display: The Emperor, too, was greatly distressed, and being told that Kashiwagi was not expected to live much longer, Where one beauty’s in possession There no other can take hold.
pairs.genji_half: The Emperor, too, was greatly distressed, and being told that Kashiwagi was not expected to live much longer,
pairs.quijote_half: Where one beauty’s in possession There no other can take hold.
pairs.genji_source:
The Emperor, too, was greatly distressed, and being told that Kashiwagi
was not expected to live much longer, he thought he might safely confer
upon him the rank of Counsellor Extraordinary.
pairs.quijote_source:
Painting that is laid on painting
  Maketh no display or show;
Where one beauty’s in possession
  There no other can take hold.

pairs.display: Yūgiri, seeing that his father was not at all inclined to take him into his confidence, But rather let thy laughter gaily ring And prove my death to be thy festival.
pairs.genji_half: Yūgiri, seeing that his father was not at all inclined to take him into his confidence,
pairs.quijote_half: But rather let thy laughter gaily ring And prove my death to be thy festival.
pairs.genji_source:
Yūgiri, seeing that
his father was not at all inclined to take him into his confidence,
thought he had better postpone the attempt (long overdue) to deliver
Kashiwagi’s cryptic death-bed message.

pairs.display: Now that all the Emperor’s time was divided between the two princesses of his retinue, we cursed the soldier’s finery and railed at the carelessness of Leandra’s father.
pairs.genji_half: Now that all the Emperor’s time was divided between the two princesses of his retinue,
pairs.quijote_half: we cursed the soldier’s finery and railed at the carelessness of Leandra’s father.
pairs.genji_source:
Now that all the Emperor’s time was divided between the two princesses
of his retinue, Prince Hyōbukyō had given up all idea of presenting
his second daughter at Court.
pairs.quijote_source:
Our melancholy grew greater, our patience grew
less; we cursed the soldier’s finery and railed at the carelessness of
Leandra’s father.

pairs.display: No doubt it was meant as a joke; Friend to friend the bug, etc.
pairs.genji_half: No doubt it was meant as a joke;
pairs.quijote_half: Friend to friend the bug, etc.
pairs.genji_source:
No doubt it was meant
as a joke; but Kashiwagi found it quite impossible to be amused.
pairs.quijote_source:
And some one else has sung--

Friend to friend the bug, etc.

pairs.display: I hear, by the way, that she is pretty; such a piece of luck as is offered you now?
pairs.genji_half: I hear, by the way, that she is pretty;
pairs.quijote_half: such a piece of luck as is offered you now?
pairs.genji_source:
I hear, by the way, that she is pretty;
after Nyosan, she was certainly her father’s favourite.’
pairs.quijote_source:
Do you think Fortune will offer you behind every stone such a piece
of luck as is offered you now?

pairs.display: Yoshikiyo was son of the Governor of Harima in its own Portuguese tongue, but we have not as yet acted them.
pairs.genji_half: Yoshikiyo was son of the Governor of Harima
pairs.quijote_half: in its own Portuguese tongue, but we have not as yet acted them.
pairs.genji_source:
Yoshikiyo was son of the Governor of Harima and
had courted the Lady of Akashi.
pairs.quijote_source:
We have prepared two eclogues, one by the famous poet
Garcilasso, the other by the most excellent Camoens, in its own
Portuguese tongue, but we have not as yet acted them.

pairs.display: In point of fact, the person to whom Lady Tamakatsura had been thus yet still triumphant was your fall
pairs.genji_half: In point of fact, the person to whom Lady Tamakatsura had been thus
pairs.quijote_half: yet still triumphant was your fall
pairs.genji_source:
In point of fact, the person to whom Lady Tamakatsura had been thus
unceremoniously compelled to give place was none other than her
mother’s faithful maid, Ukon!
pairs.quijote_source:
Though vanquished, yet ye earned the victor’s crown:
Though mourned, yet still triumphant was your fall

pairs.display: From time to time she made short visits to her palace, Or even sit beside thy bed And scratch thy dusty poll!
pairs.genji_half: From time to time she made short visits to her palace,
pairs.quijote_half: Or even sit beside thy bed And scratch thy dusty poll!
pairs.genji_source:
From time to
time she made short visits to her palace, but so secretly that Genji
did not hear of it.
pairs.quijote_source:
Thy mighty arms enfold,
Or even sit beside thy bed
    And scratch thy dusty poll!

pairs.display: Such as Buddha’s birthday, Māyā’s birthday, Buddha’s Nirvāna day, etc. The Heir Apparent, for after all we were born there and it is our natural fatherland.
pairs.genji_half: Such as Buddha’s birthday, Māyā’s birthday, Buddha’s Nirvāna day, etc. The Heir Apparent,
pairs.quijote_half: for after all we were born there and it is our natural fatherland.
pairs.genji_source:
Such as Buddha’s birthday, Māyā’s birthday, Buddha’s Nirvāna day,
etc.

 The Heir Apparent, Genji’s son by Fujitsubo, supposed to be the
old Emperor’s child.
pairs.quijote_source:
Wherever we are we weep for Spain; for
after all we were born there and it is our natural fatherland.

pairs.display: Genji now sent for him and certain it remains No God loves cruelty; then who ordains This penance that enthrals while it torments?
pairs.genji_half: Genji now sent for him
pairs.quijote_half: and certain it remains No God loves cruelty; then who ordains This penance that enthrals while it torments?
pairs.genji_source:
Genji now
sent for him and bade him perform the ceremony of Purification.
pairs.quijote_source:
But if Love be a God, it follows thence
  That he knows all, and certain it remains
  No God loves cruelty; then who ordains
This penance that enthrals while it torments?

pairs.genji_half: --- ﻿The Project Gutenberg eBook of A wreath of cloud Title: A wreath of cloud,
pairs.quijote_half: who received and treated him with great cordiality, knowing the affection her husband felt for him.
pairs.genji_source:

﻿The Project Gutenberg eBook of A wreath of cloud
Title: A wreath of cloud, Being the third part of 'The tale of Genji'
Author: Murasaki Shikibu
Translator:
pairs.quijote_source:
The
next day he went to dine with his friend, and was welcomed by Camilla,
who received and treated him with great cordiality, knowing the affection
her husband felt for him.

pairs.display: Worse than the general scandal and discredit, worse than the spectacle of Nyosan’s misery and entreaty, and repeated to him this sonnet on the fable or story of Pyramus and Thisbe.
