pairs.display: It is about Nyosan and the humours of those you take after!
pairs.genji_half: It is about Nyosan
pairs.quijote_half: and the humours of those you take after!
pairs.genji_source: It is about Nyosan that I am chiefly troubled.
pairs.quijote_source:
A plague on your humours and the humours of those you take
after!

pairs.display: Tired of merely hearing news about her health and employments, he made friends with a certain Kojijū, ye virgins of from fourteen to fifteen?
pairs.genji_half: Tired of merely hearing news about her health and employments, he made friends with a certain Kojijū,
pairs.quijote_half: ye virgins of from fourteen to fifteen?
pairs.genji_source:
Tired of
merely hearing news about her health and employments, he made friends
with a certain Kojijū, the little daughter of Nyosan’s nurse, and
tried to persuade her to carry messages.
pairs.quijote_source:
Why ye pursue her, ye virgins of from
fourteen to fifteen?

pairs.display: While he spoke she kept on asking herself who he was, he said he had about twenty ducats in a leather purse in his bosom.
pairs.genji_half: While he spoke she kept on asking herself who he was,
pairs.quijote_half: he said he had about twenty ducats in a leather purse in his bosom.
pairs.genji_source:
While he spoke she kept on asking herself who
he was, and it was only gradually that it dawned upon her he could not
very well be any one but Kashiwagi.
pairs.quijote_source:
The governor on this asked him if he had any money in silver about him;
he said he had about twenty ducats in a leather purse in his bosom.

pairs.display: Always, for years past, it had been for Kashiwagi that he had sent whenever either and keep watch, that they surprise you not.
pairs.genji_half: Always, for years past, it had been for Kashiwagi that he had sent whenever either
pairs.quijote_half: and keep watch, that they surprise you not.
pairs.genji_source:
Always, for years past, it had been for Kashiwagi
that he had sent whenever either pleasure or business demanded the
presence of some chosen friend whom he liked and could trust.
pairs.quijote_source:
It behoves you to be on the alert and keep watch,
that they surprise you not.

pairs.display: Kojijū then told him for his offers as well as she could, took leave of him in tears.
pairs.genji_half: Kojijū then told him
pairs.quijote_half: for his offers as well as she could, took leave of him in tears.
pairs.genji_source:
Kojijū then told him Nyosan
had received his message.
pairs.quijote_source:
Claudia
would not on any account allow him to accompany her; and thanking him for
his offers as well as she could, took leave of him in tears.

pairs.display: To Nyosan, curiously enough, to shift from place to place.
pairs.genji_half: To Nyosan, curiously enough,
pairs.quijote_half: to shift from place to place.
pairs.genji_source: To Nyosan, curiously enough, he could see no resemblance.
pairs.quijote_source:
They slept standing,
breaking their slumbers to shift from place to place.

pairs.display: It appalled the Lady of Akashi to discover what manifold varieties of suffering and gentleness and modesty abide with all the virtues that can confer praise, honour, and happiness upon a woman.
pairs.genji_half: It appalled the Lady of Akashi to discover what manifold varieties of suffering
pairs.quijote_half: and gentleness and modesty abide with all the virtues that can confer praise, honour, and happiness upon a woman.
pairs.genji_source:
It appalled the Lady of Akashi to
discover what manifold varieties of suffering one can be called upon
to endure at one and the same time.
pairs.quijote_source:
In short, as Camilla is the essence of all beauty, so is she
the treasure-house where purity dwells, and gentleness and modesty abide
with all the virtues that can confer praise, honour, and happiness upon a
woman.

pairs.display: On the third day of the first month, being a day of the Rat, while the living who have been rewarded may be summed up with three figures.
pairs.genji_half: On the third day of the first month, being a day of the Rat,
pairs.quijote_half: while the living who have been rewarded may be summed up with three figures.
pairs.genji_source:
On the third day of the first month, being a day of
the Rat, Tamakatsura determined to take upon herself the customary
presentation of spring shoots.
pairs.quijote_source:
No doubt you will reply that there can be no comparison, that the dead
cannot be numbered, while the living who have been rewarded may be summed
up with three figures.

pairs.display: The secrecy was not however as well maintained as he could have desired; even Suzaku, and stateliness they conducted him into another room, where there was a sumptuous table laid with but four covers.
pairs.genji_half: The secrecy was not however as well maintained as he could have desired; even Suzaku,
pairs.quijote_half: and stateliness they conducted him into another room, where there was a sumptuous table laid with but four covers.
pairs.genji_source:
The secrecy was
not however as well maintained as he could have desired; even Suzaku,
in his secluded apartments, heard the story and determined to put his
own collection at the service of Princess Akikonomu.
pairs.quijote_source:
They placed him in the midst of
them, and with much pomp and stateliness they conducted him into another
room, where there was a sumptuous table laid with but four covers.

pairs.display: Hearing this, the Emperor did not send for them to come to the Palace, virtuous, pure-minded and prudent, what is it that thou seekest?
pairs.genji_half: Hearing this, the Emperor did not send for them to come to the Palace,
pairs.quijote_half: virtuous, pure-minded and prudent, what is it that thou seekest?
pairs.genji_source:
Hearing this, the Emperor did not send for them to
come to the Palace, because of the law against the admission of
foreigners which was made by the Emperor Uda.
pairs.quijote_source:
Then, if thou knowest that thou hast a wife, modest, virtuous,
pure-minded and prudent, what is it that thou seekest?

pairs.display: Everyone seemed to make a great deal of fuss about Princess Aoi, advance him, and reward him.
pairs.genji_half: Everyone seemed to make a great deal of fuss about Princess Aoi,
pairs.quijote_half: advance him, and reward him.
pairs.genji_source:
Everyone seemed to make a great deal of fuss about
Princess Aoi, his betrothed; but he could see nothing nice about her.
pairs.quijote_source:
The servant sleeps and the master lies awake
thinking how he is to feed him, advance him, and reward him.

pairs.display: It happened that Genji went outside for a little while and Koremitsu, who was with him, recited an acrostic verse in which and during the three days he lived after that on which he made his will he fainted away very often.
pairs.genji_half: It happened that Genji went outside for a little while and Koremitsu, who was with him, recited an acrostic verse in which
pairs.quijote_half: and during the three days he lived after that on which he made his will he fainted away very often.
pairs.genji_source:
It happened that Genji went outside for a little while
and Koremitsu, who was with him, recited an acrostic verse in which he
hinted that beneath the pine-trees of Sumiyoshi a less solemn stillness
now prevailed than when the Gods first ruled on earth.
pairs.quijote_source:
All were in a flutter
and made haste to relieve him, and during the three days he lived after
that on which he made his will he fainted away very often.

pairs.display: Rider after rider again as the procession went by would pause in front of Aoi’s coach with an epitaph which was to be to this effect: Beneath the stone before your eyes
pairs.genji_half: Rider after rider again as the procession went by would pause in front of Aoi’s coach
pairs.quijote_half: with an epitaph which was to be to this effect: Beneath the stone before your eyes
pairs.genji_source:
Rider after rider again as the procession
went by would pause in front of Aoi’s coach and salute her with the
deepest respect.
pairs.quijote_source:
They closed the grave with a heavy
stone until a slab was ready which Ambrosio said he meant to have
prepared, with an epitaph which was to be to this effect:

Beneath the stone before your eyes

pairs.display: There was among these gentlewomen a little orphan girl and inflicted some outrage upon them.
pairs.genji_half: There was among these gentlewomen a little orphan girl
pairs.quijote_half: and inflicted some outrage upon them.
pairs.genji_source:
There was among these gentlewomen a little orphan girl who had been
Aoi’s favourite among all her maids.
pairs.quijote_source:
Depend upon it, by
this time those dogs would have overtaken them and inflicted some outrage
upon them.

pairs.display: The Akashi Princess was also in residence at the New Palace, if they be all ungrateful!
pairs.genji_half: The Akashi Princess was also in residence at the New Palace,
pairs.quijote_half: if they be all ungrateful!
pairs.genji_source:
The Akashi
Princess was also in residence at the New Palace, with her little boy,
Niou, and her second child, also a boy.
pairs.quijote_source:
A plague on all the knights-errant in the
world, if they be all ungrateful!

pairs.display: He is a likely looking boy, and I might make him my body-servant, and he talked to them about himself very naturally and in very well-chosen language.
pairs.genji_half: He is a likely looking boy, and I might make him my body-servant,
pairs.quijote_half: and he talked to them about himself very naturally and in very well-chosen language.
pairs.genji_source:
He is a likely looking boy, and I might make him my
body-servant, or even recommend him to the Emperor.’
pairs.quijote_source:
They were very
cordially received by him; they asked him after his health, and he talked
to them about himself very naturally and in very well-chosen language.

pairs.display: We too, you may remember, were planning to give my sister to Genji for wisdom, they’ll not fail to get me one for ingenuity.
pairs.genji_half: We too, you may remember, were planning to give my sister to Genji
pairs.quijote_half: for wisdom, they’ll not fail to get me one for ingenuity.
pairs.genji_source:
We too,
you may remember, were planning to give my sister to Genji when we were
outwitted by this hasty wedding, of which till the last minute no one
was given the slightest intimation.
pairs.quijote_source:
And if they don’t get me a name for wisdom,
they’ll not fail to get me one for ingenuity.

pairs.display: If you will do for this new-comer what you have been doing what cracked head, that thou canst not forget that jest?
pairs.genji_half: If you will do for this new-comer what you have been doing
pairs.quijote_half: what cracked head, that thou canst not forget that jest?
pairs.genji_source:
If you
will do for this new-comer what you have been doing for Prince Yūgiri,
I shall be deeply thankful to you.
pairs.quijote_source:
What lame leg hast thou got by it, what broken rib, what
cracked head, that thou canst not forget that jest?

pairs.display: He did not understand what had happened, but seeing the servants all wringing their hands and left them filled with admiration not only at his remarks but at his firm and sensible resolution.
pairs.genji_half: He did not understand what had happened, but seeing the servants all wringing their hands
pairs.quijote_half: and left them filled with admiration not only at his remarks but at his firm and sensible resolution.
pairs.genji_source:
He
did not understand what had happened, but seeing the servants all
wringing their hands and the Emperor himself continually weeping, he
felt that it must have been something very terrible.
pairs.quijote_source:
They all embraced him, and he with tears embraced all of them,
and left them filled with admiration not only at his remarks but at his
firm and sensible resolution.

pairs.display: The warmth of the old ex-Emperor’s messages and his evident personal distress at Aoi’s death were indeed very flattering and shoes to match for holidays, while on week-days he made a brave figure in his best homespun.
pairs.genji_half: The warmth of the old ex-Emperor’s messages and his evident personal distress at Aoi’s death were indeed very flattering
pairs.quijote_half: and shoes to match for holidays, while on week-days he made a brave figure in his best homespun.
pairs.genji_source:
The warmth of the old ex-Emperor’s messages and his evident personal
distress at Aoi’s death were indeed very flattering and mingled a
certain feeling of gratification with her father’s perpetual weeping.
pairs.quijote_source:
The rest of it
went in a doublet of fine cloth and velvet breeches and shoes to match
for holidays, while on week-days he made a brave figure in his best
homespun.

pairs.display: As it was known that the Emperor had previously been very much in love with her, and hollows of the trees, offering without usance the plenteous produce of their fragrant toil to every hand.
pairs.genji_half: As it was known that the Emperor had previously been very much in love with her,
pairs.quijote_half: and hollows of the trees, offering without usance the plenteous produce of their fragrant toil to every hand.
pairs.genji_source:
As it was known that the Emperor had previously been very much
in love with her, no surprise was felt when he began immediately to
treat her as a full lady-in-waiting.
pairs.quijote_source:
The busy and sagacious bees fixed their republic in the clefts
of the rocks and hollows of the trees, offering without usance the
plenteous produce of their fragrant toil to every hand.

pairs.display: The Emperor had been and could not guess what had led them to make so extraordinary a change.
pairs.genji_half: The Emperor had been
pairs.quijote_half: and could not guess what had led them to make so extraordinary a change.
pairs.genji_source:
The Emperor had been somewhat
nervous about this interview.
pairs.quijote_source:
When the villagers saw the two scholars
so unexpectedly appearing in shepherd’s dress, they were lost in wonder,
and could not guess what had led them to make so extraordinary a change.

pairs.display: The boy who had sung the Ballad of Takasago so prettily some years ago, trees, shrubs, and bushes,
pairs.genji_half: The boy who had sung the Ballad of Takasago so prettily some years ago,
pairs.quijote_half: trees, shrubs, and bushes,
pairs.genji_source:
The boy who had sung the Ballad of Takasago so prettily
some years ago, was already installed as one of the Emperor’s pages and
was thought to be doing very well.
pairs.quijote_source: Ye green things all, trees, shrubs, and bushes,

pairs.display: then at the Emperor’s death of my life!
pairs.genji_half: then at the Emperor’s
pairs.quijote_half: death of my life!
pairs.genji_source: then at the Emperor’s Palace without your taking offence.
pairs.quijote_source:
robber of my
glory, death of my life!

pairs.display: But Lady Aoi’s death has removed the one insurmountable obstacle which before made it out and that when the hunters wish to take it, they make use of this artifice.
pairs.genji_half: But Lady Aoi’s death has removed the one insurmountable obstacle which before made it out
pairs.quijote_half: and that when the hunters wish to take it, they make use of this artifice.
pairs.genji_source:
But Lady Aoi’s death has removed the one insurmountable
obstacle which before made it out of the question that any person of
consequence should offer to this Prince his daughter’s hand.
pairs.quijote_source:
Naturalists tell us that the ermine is a little animal which has a fur of
purest white, and that when the hunters wish to take it, they make use of
this artifice.

pairs.display: and he made many suggestions that now, even though thou repentest, thou canst not help being mine.
pairs.genji_half: and he made many suggestions
pairs.quijote_half: that now, even though thou repentest, thou canst not help being mine.
pairs.genji_source:
and he made many suggestions for
Tamakatsura’s polite education.
pairs.quijote_source:
It was thy will to make me thine, and
thou didst so follow thy will, that now, even though thou repentest, thou
canst not help being mine.

pairs.display: Ateki of course knew and manifest proof of their gallantry and resolution, and how sturdily they had defended themselves and held their post.
pairs.genji_half: Ateki of course knew
pairs.quijote_half: and manifest proof of their gallantry and resolution, and how sturdily they had defended themselves and held their post.
pairs.genji_source:
Ateki of course knew the secret of
        Tamakatsura’s birth.
pairs.quijote_source:
Of three hundred that remained alive not one was
taken unwounded, a clear and manifest proof of their gallantry and
resolution, and how sturdily they had defended themselves and held their
post.

pairs.display: She did not want except that of coming across some vessel out on a roving expedition.
pairs.genji_half: She did not want
pairs.quijote_half: except that of coming across some vessel out on a roving expedition.
pairs.genji_source:
She did not want to become the Emperor’s
concubine.
pairs.quijote_source:
All this was done very promptly, and under sail we ran
more than eight miles an hour without any fear, except that of coming
across some vessel out on a roving expedition.

pairs.display: The Emperor was impatient to get the matter settled, and her various lovers pressed their so likewise he will restore you if you trust in him.
pairs.genji_half: The Emperor was impatient to get the matter settled, and her various lovers pressed their
pairs.quijote_half: so likewise he will restore you if you trust in him.
pairs.genji_source:
The Emperor was impatient to get the matter settled, and her various
lovers pressed their claims more vehemently than ever upon those whom
they imagined to be responsible for her decision.
pairs.quijote_source:
Have strong hope
and trust in him, for as he has restored me to my original condition, so
likewise he will restore you if you trust in him.

pairs.display: His Majesty has an undoubted partiality for her; and any one in that position, people think of me?
pairs.genji_half: His Majesty has an undoubted partiality for her; and any one in that position,
pairs.quijote_half: people think of me?
pairs.genji_source:
His Majesty has an
undoubted partiality for her; and any one in that position, unless
backed up by the most powerful family connections, is apt to have a
very disagreeable time.
pairs.quijote_source:
What do
the common people think of me?

pairs.display: If he had designs of any kind, going to look for?
pairs.genji_half: If he had designs of any kind,
pairs.quijote_half: going to look for?
pairs.genji_source:
If he had designs of
any kind, they were not upon Nyosan but upon her cat.
pairs.quijote_source:
Then what are
you going to look for?

pairs.display: But lately Suzaku had just as if, as soon as day broke, they were not going to break one another’s heads.
pairs.genji_half: But lately Suzaku had
pairs.quijote_half: just as if, as soon as day broke, they were not going to break one another’s heads.
pairs.genji_source:
But lately Suzaku had shown nothing
but affability....
pairs.quijote_source:
In the meantime, they had seated themselves together on the hard ground
peaceably and sociably, just as if, as soon as day broke, they were not
going to break one another’s heads.

pairs.display: But both Suzaku and her brother the present Emperor evidently counted upon me to take charge the best for me.
pairs.genji_half: But both Suzaku and her brother the present Emperor evidently counted upon me to take charge
pairs.quijote_half: the best for me.
pairs.genji_source:
But both Suzaku and
her brother the present Emperor evidently counted upon me to take
charge at any rate of her zithern lessons, and I had not the
heart to disappoint them.
pairs.quijote_source: And yet, thought were the best for me.

pairs.display: The ceremony accordingly took place on the appointed day; prayers were said in the usual Fifty temples, a way whate’er befall.
pairs.genji_half: The ceremony accordingly took place on the appointed day; prayers were said in the usual Fifty temples,
pairs.quijote_half: a way whate’er befall.
pairs.genji_source:
The ceremony accordingly took
place on the appointed day; prayers were said in the usual Fifty
temples, and in Suzaku’s own retreat the Scripture of the Great Sun
Buddha was solemnly recited.
pairs.quijote_source: For love will find a way whate’er befall.

pairs.display: To Suzaku, had not the affairs of this world been by now of shadowy import to him, bears herself--and her shape!
pairs.genji_half: To Suzaku, had not the affairs of this world been by now of shadowy import to him,
pairs.quijote_half: bears herself--and her shape!
pairs.genji_source:
To Suzaku, had not the affairs of this world been by now of shadowy
import to him, the tragic outcome of all his paternal solicitude
would indeed have been a shattering blow.
pairs.quijote_source: See how bravely she bears herself--and her shape!

pairs.display: In his final instructions to Genji concerning the management of public affairs he recurred again but the lost ass never gave them an answer or even the sign of one.
pairs.genji_half: In his final instructions to Genji concerning the management of public affairs he recurred again
pairs.quijote_half: but the lost ass never gave them an answer or even the sign of one.
pairs.genji_source:
In his final instructions to Genji concerning
the management of public affairs he recurred again and again to the
question of the Heir Apparent and the importance of giving him due
protection and advice.
pairs.quijote_source:
In this way, doubling the brays at every step, they made the complete
circuit of the forest, but the lost ass never gave them an answer or even
the sign of one.

pairs.display: Tō no Chūjō addressed such words to him; and though there was no direct allusion to the trouble over Kumoi, and she is left hanging in the air, unable to reach the ground.
pairs.genji_half: Tō no Chūjō addressed such words to him; and though there was no direct allusion to the trouble over Kumoi,
pairs.quijote_half: and she is left hanging in the air, unable to reach the ground.
pairs.genji_source:
Tō no Chūjō addressed such words to him; and though
there was no direct allusion to the trouble over Kumoi, he could not
help feeling that they were meant as a definite hint of encouragement.
pairs.quijote_source:
But ah! unhappy lady, the edge of
her petticoat has caught on one of the bars of the balcony and she is
left hanging in the air, unable to reach the ground.

pairs.display: His sufferings became more and more acute, and revenge themselves and go about perpetually making trombones of their swords in every petty quarrel!
pairs.genji_half: His sufferings became more and more acute,
pairs.quijote_half: and revenge themselves and go about perpetually making trombones of their swords in every petty quarrel!
pairs.genji_source:
His sufferings became more and more
acute, despite the fact that continual services of intercession were
held both in the Palace and at Kōkiden’s house.
pairs.quijote_source:
It would be a
nice business indeed if all these illustrious cities were to take huff
and revenge themselves and go about perpetually making trombones of their
swords in every petty quarrel!

pairs.display: Genji read the poem and was touched by it; for he knew that only under the stress at, heart of butter-paste?
pairs.genji_half: Genji read the poem and was touched by it; for he knew that only under the stress
pairs.quijote_half: at, heart of butter-paste?
pairs.genji_source:
Genji read the poem and was touched by it; for he knew that only under
the stress of great emotion would she have allowed this note of sadness
to tinge a New Year poem.
pairs.quijote_source:
What art thou weeping at, heart of
butter-paste?

pairs.display: The two women exchanged addresses and set out upon their different ways: Ukon to a little house Genji had given her, of her woe resolved.
pairs.genji_half: The two women exchanged addresses and set out upon their different ways: Ukon to a little house Genji had given her,
pairs.quijote_half: of her woe resolved.
pairs.genji_source:
The two women exchanged addresses
and set out upon their different ways: Ukon to a little house Genji
had given her, not far away from his new palace; the others to their
lodgings in the Ninth Ward.
pairs.quijote_source: Thus have the authors of her woe resolved.

pairs.display: And now Genji, who while he alone was there had supported and encouraged the weeping maid-servant, a way of disenchanting!
pairs.genji_half: And now Genji, who while he alone was there had supported and encouraged the weeping maid-servant,
pairs.quijote_half: a way of disenchanting!
pairs.genji_source:
And now Genji, who
while he alone was there had supported and encouraged the weeping
maid-servant, relieved at last by Koremitsu could contain himself no
longer, and suddenly realizing again the terrible thing that had
befallen him he burst into uncontrollable weeping.
pairs.quijote_source: The devil take such a way of disenchanting!

pairs.display: Her attendants had no means of knowing that anything out of the ordinary had happened, and some will say they are white and others will say they are black.
pairs.genji_half: Her attendants had no means of knowing that anything out of the ordinary had happened,
pairs.quijote_half: and some will say they are white and others will say they are black.
pairs.genji_source:
Her attendants had no means of
knowing that anything out of the ordinary had happened, for it had
always been Genji’s habit to go in and out of her room just as he
chose.
pairs.quijote_source:
Say
nothing of this to anyone; for, make thy affairs public, and some will
say they are white and others will say they are black.

pairs.display: Again and again, under pressure from Kōkiden or the Minister of the Right, whereby all his plans were overthrown and swept away, as will be seen farther on.
pairs.genji_half: Again and again, under pressure from Kōkiden or the Minister of the Right,
pairs.quijote_half: whereby all his plans were overthrown and swept away, as will be seen farther on.
pairs.genji_source:
Again and again, under pressure from
Kōkiden or the Minister of the Right, he allowed public measures to
be taken of which he did not really in the least approve.
pairs.quijote_source:
And so the night’s
round came to an end, and a couple of days later the government, whereby
all his plans were overthrown and swept away, as will be seen farther on.

pairs.display: Genji was very much disappointed and after two or three days he sent for Koremitsu and gave him a letter we have before us?
pairs.genji_half: Genji was very much disappointed and after two or three days he sent for Koremitsu and gave him a letter
pairs.quijote_half: we have before us?
pairs.genji_source:
Genji was very much
disappointed and after two or three days he sent for Koremitsu and
gave him a letter for the nun, telling him at the same time to
find out whatever he could from Shōnagon, the child’s nurse.
pairs.quijote_source:
Seest thou this gentleman we have
before us?

pairs.display: She thought that he had reluctantly left the New Palace merely out of pity not speak so low
pairs.genji_half: She thought that he had reluctantly left the New Palace merely out of pity
pairs.quijote_half: not speak so low
pairs.genji_source:
She thought that he had reluctantly left the New Palace merely out of
pity for her, and was worried at being obliged to spend so much time
out of Nyosan’s company.
pairs.quijote_source: The unlucky wight did not speak so low

pairs.display: Murasaki had never been openly hostile; gentlemen bearing thee company!
pairs.genji_half: Murasaki had never been openly hostile;
pairs.quijote_half: gentlemen bearing thee company!
pairs.genji_source:
Murasaki had never been openly hostile; but she had
certainly suffered very much.
pairs.quijote_source:
Why, look at these
gentlemen bearing thee company!

pairs.display: These were so appropriate that they could hardly have been produced on the spur of the moment, where a vast multitude stood waiting for them, eager to see what they brought back.
pairs.genji_half: These were so appropriate that they could hardly have been produced on the spur of the moment,
pairs.quijote_half: where a vast multitude stood waiting for them, eager to see what they brought back.
pairs.genji_source:
These were so appropriate that they
could hardly have been produced on the spur of the moment, and it
almost seemed as though some hint of Murasaki’s intention had reached
the Empress’s quarters beforehand.
pairs.quijote_source:
The other two
galleys now joined company and all four returned with the prize to the
beach, where a vast multitude stood waiting for them, eager to see what
they brought back.

pairs.display: As for you, Niou, rashness is not valour?
pairs.genji_half: As for you, Niou,
pairs.quijote_half: rashness is not valour?
pairs.genji_source: As for you, Niou, you’re a little rascal.
pairs.quijote_source: Knowest thou not that rashness is not valour?

pairs.display: Remembering that this was the end of the year, little Niou went scampering about saying every pastoral odds and ends!
pairs.genji_half: Remembering that this was the end of the year, little Niou went scampering about saying every
pairs.quijote_half: pastoral odds and ends!
pairs.genji_source:
Remembering that this was the end of the year, little Niou went
scampering about saying every one must do something to scare away
the demons, and asking what noise he might make.
pairs.quijote_source:
What messes, creams, garlands,
pastoral odds and ends!

pairs.display: Most of the ex-Emperor’s pictures had belonged to his mother, the Empress Kōkiden; been amazed at this?
pairs.genji_half: Most of the ex-Emperor’s pictures had belonged to his mother, the Empress Kōkiden;
pairs.quijote_half: been amazed at this?
pairs.genji_source:
Most of the ex-Emperor’s pictures had belonged to his mother, the
Empress Kōkiden; unfortunately a considerable part of her collection
had however come into the possession of Lady Chūjō, who was her
grand-daughter.
pairs.quijote_source: Who would not have been amazed at this?

pairs.display: The residence of the ex-Emperor to make him quail.
pairs.genji_half: The residence of the ex-Emperor
pairs.quijote_half: to make him quail.
pairs.genji_source: The residence of the ex-Emperor and his mother, Kōkiden.
pairs.quijote_source: In that last hour, to make him quail.

pairs.display: Koremitsu, who was near his coach, overheard these words, and regarding them as a command to him to produce writing materials (a duty that he overthrew the virtuous resolves of Camilla and won the triumph he least expected and most longed for.
pairs.genji_half: Koremitsu, who was near his coach, overheard these words, and regarding them as a command to him to produce writing materials (a duty
pairs.quijote_half: that he overthrew the virtuous resolves of Camilla and won the triumph he least expected and most longed for.
pairs.genji_source:
Koremitsu, who was near his coach, overheard these words, and
regarding them as a command to him to produce writing materials (a
duty for which he was often in request) he whipped out a short-handled
pen from the folds of his dress and as soon as Genji’s coach came to a
standstill handed it in to him.
pairs.quijote_source:
He wept, he
entreated, he promised, he flattered, he importuned, he pretended with so
much feeling and apparent sincerity, that he overthrew the virtuous
resolves of Camilla and won the triumph he least expected and most longed
for.

pairs.display: The fact that, among the many women upon whom Genji had bestowed his favours, over the fence or wall of the garden, and were gathering the fruit though it was not yet ripe.
pairs.genji_half: The fact that, among the many women upon whom Genji had bestowed his favours,
pairs.quijote_half: over the fence or wall of the garden, and were gathering the fruit though it was not yet ripe.
pairs.genji_source:
The fact that, among the many women upon whom Genji had
bestowed his favours, it was Murasaki who had been chosen to be the
mistress of his house, gave to Hyōbukyō, as her father, a certain
worldly prestige.
pairs.quijote_source:
While we were still engaged in this conversation, a Moor came running up,
exclaiming that four Turks had leaped over the fence or wall of the
garden, and were gathering the fruit though it was not yet ripe.

pairs.display: Hearing this Myōbu determined to submit Genji’s request to her at some suitable moment when she or that of Fierabras and the bridge of Mantible, which happened in the time of Charlemagne?
pairs.genji_half: Hearing this Myōbu determined to submit Genji’s request to her at some suitable moment when she
pairs.quijote_half: or that of Fierabras and the bridge of Mantible, which happened in the time of Charlemagne?
pairs.genji_source:
Hearing this Myōbu determined to submit Genji’s request to her at some
suitable moment when she and the princess were carrying on one of
their usual uneasy conversations, with the princess’s screen-of-honour
planted between them.
pairs.quijote_source:
What wit in the world can persuade another
that the story of the Princess Floripes and Guy of Burgundy is not true,
or that of Fierabras and the bridge of Mantible, which happened in the
time of Charlemagne?

pairs.display: After the festival of the White Horse on the seventh day Genji left the Emperor’s presence at nightfall in dread of some catastrophe in consequence of his appearance at such a moment.
pairs.genji_half: After the festival of the White Horse on the seventh day Genji left the Emperor’s presence at nightfall
pairs.quijote_half: in dread of some catastrophe in consequence of his appearance at such a moment.
pairs.genji_source:
After the
festival of the White Horse on the seventh day Genji left the
Emperor’s presence at nightfall and went to his own apartments in the
Palace as though intending to stay the night there.
pairs.quijote_source:
As he approached he was recognised by everyone as the
gay Basilio, and all waited anxiously to see what would come of his
words, in dread of some catastrophe in consequence of his appearance at
such a moment.

pairs.display: Whereupon Chūjō danced the Park of Willow Flowers, giving a far more complete performance than Genji, and as he was one of those who were privy to the arrangement of the affair he knew not what to say in reply.
pairs.genji_half: Whereupon Chūjō danced the Park of Willow Flowers, giving a far more complete performance than Genji,
pairs.quijote_half: and as he was one of those who were privy to the arrangement of the affair he knew not what to say in reply.
pairs.genji_source:
Whereupon Chūjō danced the Park of Willow Flowers, giving a far more
complete performance than Genji, for no doubt he knew that he would be
called upon and had taken trouble to prepare his dance.
pairs.quijote_source:
The marshal of the field was lost in astonishment at the words of
Tosilos; and as he was one of those who were privy to the arrangement of
the affair he knew not what to say in reply.

pairs.display: He now spoke in a gentler tone, but it was evident that he meant to have his own way, helped him to bed, where they gave him something to eat and made him as comfortable as possible.
pairs.genji_half: He now spoke in a gentler tone, but it was evident that he meant to have his own way,
pairs.quijote_half: helped him to bed, where they gave him something to eat and made him as comfortable as possible.
pairs.genji_source:
He now spoke in a gentler tone, but it was evident that
he meant to have his own way, and to his wife’s consternation he began
to make the most lavish preparations for Genji’s entertainment.’
pairs.quijote_source:
And the
good wenches (for that they undoubtedly were), the housekeeper and niece,
helped him to bed, where they gave him something to eat and made him as
comfortable as possible.

pairs.display: However, it was a consolation to feel that Genji was now definitely re-embarking upon the path of prosperity, nor arms, nor shields quartered or whole, nor vair azure or bedevilled.
pairs.genji_half: However, it was a consolation to feel that Genji was now definitely re-embarking upon the path of prosperity,
pairs.quijote_half: nor arms, nor shields quartered or whole, nor vair azure or bedevilled.
pairs.genji_source:
However, it was a consolation to feel
that Genji was now definitely re-embarking upon the path of prosperity,
and that his partiality, should it continue, would be in the future
even more valuable than before.
pairs.quijote_source:
Look, there is no giant, nor knight, nor cats, nor arms, nor
shields quartered or whole, nor vair azure or bedevilled.

pairs.display: Among the gentlewomen present were some who had served the old Emperor his father and deserves to be valued most.
pairs.genji_half: Among the gentlewomen present were some who had served the old Emperor his father
pairs.quijote_half: and deserves to be valued most.
pairs.genji_source:
Among the gentlewomen present were some who had served the old Emperor
his father and these old ladies, who had always taken his side, now
pressed round him chattering and weeping.
pairs.quijote_source:
And then it is plain that whatever
costs most is valued and deserves to be valued most.

pairs.display: Murasaki while she listened could not but remember how particularly unhappy she had been just at the very time and thus, nature combined with art, and art with nature, will produce a perfect poet.
pairs.genji_half: Murasaki while she listened could not but remember how particularly unhappy she had been just at the very time
pairs.quijote_half: and thus, nature combined with art, and art with nature, will produce a perfect poet.
pairs.genji_source:
Murasaki while she listened could not but remember how
particularly unhappy she had been just at the very time when the
episodes which Genji was now recalling with such relish were taking
place at Akashi.
pairs.quijote_source:
The
reason is, that art does not surpass nature, but only brings it to
perfection; and thus, nature combined with art, and art with nature, will
produce a perfect poet.

pairs.display: Her father, seeing the house full of the servants and retainers whom Genji had sent from the Capital, what bowels of brass, what a soul of mortar!
pairs.genji_half: Her father, seeing the house full of the servants and retainers whom Genji had sent from the Capital,
pairs.quijote_half: what bowels of brass, what a soul of mortar!
pairs.genji_source:
Her father, seeing the
house full of the servants and retainers whom Genji had sent from the
Capital, could not deny to himself that here indeed was the fulfilment
of his every dream and prayer.
pairs.quijote_source:
What a heart of marble,
what bowels of brass, what a soul of mortar!

pairs.display: The wet-nurse and other young persons whom at the time of the child’s birth Genji had sent while shunning each extreme, The sparing hand, the over-free, Therein consists, so wise men deem, The virtue Liberality.
pairs.genji_half: The wet-nurse and other young persons whom at the time of the child’s birth Genji had sent
pairs.quijote_half: while shunning each extreme, The sparing hand, the over-free, Therein consists, so wise men deem, The virtue Liberality.
pairs.genji_source:
The wet-nurse
and other young persons whom at the time of the child’s birth Genji
had sent from the Capital were beginning to become very restive and
the prospect of the coming journey delighted them.
pairs.quijote_source:
Poetry withdrew, and on the side of Interest Liberality advanced, and
after having gone through her figures, said:

To give, while shunning each extreme,
  The sparing hand, the over-free,
Therein consists, so wise men deem,
  The virtue Liberality.

pairs.display: Here was an opening; for it was perfectly natural and proper that Genji should visit this princess, he sees with, of the sun that gives him light, of the food whereby he lives.
pairs.genji_half: Here was an opening; for it was perfectly natural and proper that Genji should visit this princess,
pairs.quijote_half: he sees with, of the sun that gives him light, of the food whereby he lives.
pairs.genji_source:
Here was an
opening; for it was perfectly natural and proper that Genji should
visit this princess, who had been his father’s favourite sister and
with whom he had himself always remained on excellent terms.
pairs.quijote_source:
For to deprive a knight-errant of his lady is to deprive him of the
eyes he sees with, of the sun that gives him light, of the food whereby
he lives.

pairs.display: But women are not supposed to know anything about Chinese literature, and I will not shock your and sell three, six, or ten thousand vassals while you would be talking about it!
pairs.genji_half: But women are not supposed to know anything about Chinese literature, and I will not shock your
pairs.quijote_half: and sell three, six, or ten thousand vassals while you would be talking about it!
pairs.genji_source:
But women are not
supposed to know anything about Chinese literature, and I will not
shock your sense of propriety by quoting any of the poems—even that by
which Genji so deeply moved his hearers.
pairs.quijote_source:
Not unless you go to sleep and haven’t the wit or
skill to turn things to account and sell three, six, or ten thousand
vassals while you would be talking about it!

pairs.display: And now that all was ready, it seemed to him impossible to instal Tamakatsura in his household for his mistress Chloris; for as Camilla did not know her, he might safely say what he liked.
pairs.genji_half: And now that all was ready, it seemed to him impossible to instal Tamakatsura in his household
pairs.quijote_half: for his mistress Chloris; for as Camilla did not know her, he might safely say what he liked.
pairs.genji_source:
And now that all was ready, it seemed to him impossible
to instal Tamakatsura in his household without revealing to Murasaki
the whole truth about the girl’s identity and his own dealings with her
mother.
pairs.quijote_source:
The next day as the three were at table Anselmo asked Lothario to recite
something of what he had composed for his mistress Chloris; for as
Camilla did not know her, he might safely say what he liked.

pairs.display: By far the most passionate and profuse of her correspondents seemed to be Prince Sochi no Miya, since she had not enough at the time she had most need of it to rid herself of the life she so much loathed.
pairs.genji_half: By far the most passionate and profuse of her correspondents seemed to be Prince Sochi no Miya,
pairs.quijote_half: since she had not enough at the time she had most need of it to rid herself of the life she so much loathed.
pairs.genji_source:
By far the most
passionate and profuse of her correspondents seemed to be Prince Sochi
no Miya, and Genji smiled as he looked through the thick packet into
which that prince’s letters had been collected.
pairs.quijote_source:
To Leonela’s words Camilla added her own,
calling herself cowardly and wanting in spirit, since she had not enough
at the time she had most need of it to rid herself of the life she so
much loathed.

pairs.display: Should Genji decide to escape from his dilemma by presenting Tamakatsura at Court, they set out with him for the city, to the music of the clarions and the drums.
pairs.genji_half: Should Genji decide to escape from his dilemma by presenting Tamakatsura at Court,
pairs.quijote_half: they set out with him for the city, to the music of the clarions and the drums.
pairs.genji_source:
Should Genji decide to escape from his dilemma by presenting
Tamakatsura at Court, she might prove (thought Tō no Chūjō) a
formidable rival to his eldest daughter, who already had difficulties
enough at the Palace.
pairs.quijote_source:
The cavalier replied with words no less polite, and then, all closing in
around him, they set out with him for the city, to the music of the
clarions and the drums.

pairs.display: She showed indeed throughout the whole business (as both Genji and Tō no Chūjō were forced to admit) a good sense and backpiece to see if he had any wound, but he could perceive no blood nor any mark whatever.
pairs.genji_half: She showed indeed throughout the whole business (as both Genji and Tō no Chūjō were forced to admit) a good sense
pairs.quijote_half: and backpiece to see if he had any wound, but he could perceive no blood nor any mark whatever.
pairs.genji_source:
She showed indeed throughout the whole business (as
both Genji and Tō no Chūjō were forced to admit) a good sense and
docility that other members of her sex might have done well to imitate.
pairs.quijote_source:
Seeing this, the good man removed as well as he could his breastplate and
backpiece to see if he had any wound, but he could perceive no blood nor
any mark whatever.

pairs.display: On the tenth day of the second month there was a little rain, and boiling them a good while until it seemed to him they had come to perfection.
pairs.genji_half: On the tenth day of the second month there was a little rain,
pairs.quijote_half: and boiling them a good while until it seemed to him they had come to perfection.
pairs.genji_source:
On the tenth day of the second month there was a little rain, not more
than was needed to bring to perfection the smell and colour of the
red plum-blossom in front of Genji’s palace.
pairs.quijote_source:
To be brief, he
took the materials, of which he made a compound, mixing them all and
boiling them a good while until it seemed to him they had come to
perfection.

pairs.display: Although Nyosan would in Genji’s household grow up as one among many, and used my body as if it was an ill-washed rag, and, woe is me!
pairs.genji_half: Although Nyosan would in Genji’s household grow up as one among many,
pairs.quijote_half: and used my body as if it was an ill-washed rag, and, woe is me!
pairs.genji_source:
Although Nyosan would in Genji’s household grow up
as one among many, she would at least enjoy the advantage of having
come there as a child, an orphan whose interests Genji was pledged to
defend.
pairs.quijote_source:
Señor governor of my soul, this wicked man caught
me in the middle of the fields here and used my body as if it was an
ill-washed rag, and, woe is me!

pairs.display: It was pleasant, for instance, to feel that whatever scandals had to be investigated, Yūgiri’s name would never be involved; valour with valour, beauty with beauty, birth with birth, are always odious and unwelcome?
pairs.genji_half: It was pleasant, for instance, to feel that whatever scandals had to be investigated, Yūgiri’s name would never be involved;
pairs.quijote_half: valour with valour, beauty with beauty, birth with birth, are always odious and unwelcome?
pairs.genji_source:
It was pleasant, for instance, to feel that whatever
scandals had to be investigated, Yūgiri’s name would never be involved;
and the more so because Genji himself had suffered from the
effects of a quite opposite reputation.
pairs.quijote_source:
Is it
possible that you do not know that comparisons of wit with wit, valour
with valour, beauty with beauty, birth with birth, are always odious and
unwelcome?

pairs.display: Higekuro invented one pretext after another for her instant removal, and when each in turn had been easily and furnish him with all he wanted for his own comfort or for the journey.
pairs.genji_half: Higekuro invented one pretext after another for her instant removal, and when each in turn had been easily
pairs.quijote_half: and furnish him with all he wanted for his own comfort or for the journey.
pairs.genji_source:
Higekuro invented one pretext after another for her
instant removal, and when each in turn had been easily disposed of,
he tried to enlist the help of Tō no Chūjō and others of the Court
Council.
pairs.quijote_source:
They all agreed to this, and allowed him to go, first offering to bear
him company and furnish him with all he wanted for his own comfort or for
the journey.

pairs.display: Kumoi had left some of the children behind; but the little girls, and in a good hour be it said to you.
pairs.genji_half: Kumoi had left some of the children behind; but the little girls,
pairs.quijote_half: and in a good hour be it said to you.
pairs.genji_source:
Kumoi had left some of the children behind; but the little girls, who
were mere babies, she had taken with her to Tō no Chūjō’s palace.
pairs.quijote_source:
Go your
ways in a good hour, and in a good hour be it said to you.

pairs.display: With Nyosan, however, this and he seeing them waiting on his words in mute expectation, began thus in a pleasant quiet voice.
pairs.genji_half: With Nyosan, however, this
pairs.quijote_half: and he seeing them waiting on his words in mute expectation, began thus in a pleasant quiet voice.
pairs.genji_source:
With Nyosan, however,
this was not the case.
pairs.quijote_source:
These words made them
settle themselves in their places and preserve a deep silence, and he
seeing them waiting on his words in mute expectation, began thus in a
pleasant quiet voice.

pairs.display: It would be such for the field and market-place, who used to saddle the hack as well as handle the bill-hook.
pairs.genji_half: It would be such
pairs.quijote_half: for the field and market-place, who used to saddle the hack as well as handle the bill-hook.
pairs.genji_source:
It would
be such a comfort to Nyosan.
pairs.quijote_source:
He had in his house a housekeeper past forty, a niece under
twenty, and a lad for the field and market-place, who used to saddle the
hack as well as handle the bill-hook.

pairs.display: But Kashiwagi had been who had not yet dismounted from the ass because he could not, they ran to embrace him.
pairs.genji_half: But Kashiwagi had been
pairs.quijote_half: who had not yet dismounted from the ass because he could not, they ran to embrace him.
pairs.genji_source:
But Kashiwagi
had been something more than this.
pairs.quijote_source:
At these words they all hurried out, and when they recognised their
friend, master, and uncle, who had not yet dismounted from the ass
because he could not, they ran to embrace him.

pairs.display: Kashiwagi had but been seldom or never trodden except by the feet of goats, or of the wolves and other wild beasts that roamed around.
pairs.genji_half: Kashiwagi had but been
pairs.quijote_half: seldom or never trodden except by the feet of goats, or of the wolves and other wild beasts that roamed around.
pairs.genji_source:
Kashiwagi had but been the instrument
of Fate.
pairs.quijote_source:
He shouted
in return, asking what had brought them to that spot, seldom or never
trodden except by the feet of goats, or of the wolves and other wild
beasts that roamed around.

pairs.display: Ukon, the brother of Ki no Kami, had relinquished his office of Treasurer, and see Andres paid to the last maravedi, despite and in the teeth of all the clowns in the world.
pairs.genji_half: Ukon, the brother of Ki no Kami, had relinquished his office of Treasurer,
pairs.quijote_half: and see Andres paid to the last maravedi, despite and in the teeth of all the clowns in the world.
pairs.genji_source:
Ukon, the brother of Ki no Kami, had relinquished his office of
Treasurer, and having been appointed Quiver-bearer to His Majesty had
this year been formally invested as an officer of the 5th rank.

pairs.display: It will be remembered that at the time of Lady Akikonomu’s departure for Ise the retired Emperor Suzaku had, for she had answered him so angrily and harshly that he had no heart to say anything more to her.
pairs.genji_half: It will be remembered that at the time of Lady Akikonomu’s departure for Ise the retired Emperor Suzaku had,
pairs.quijote_half: for she had answered him so angrily and harshly that he had no heart to say anything more to her.
pairs.genji_source:
It will be remembered that at the time of Lady Akikonomu’s departure
for Ise the retired Emperor Suzaku had, when presiding at the
magnificent farewell ceremony in the Daigoku Hall, been greatly struck
with her beauty.
pairs.quijote_source:
Lothario replied that he was not disposed to go
on with the business, for she had answered him so angrily and harshly
that he had no heart to say anything more to her.

pairs.display: But it was known that Genji, Tō no Chūjō and Higekuro, the three most powerful men in the country, were her sponsors; been the utter destruction of his good name; whom Camilla received with averted countenance, though with smiles in her heart.
pairs.genji_half: But it was known that Genji, Tō no Chūjō and Higekuro, the three most powerful men in the country, were her sponsors;
pairs.quijote_half: been the utter destruction of his good name; whom Camilla received with averted countenance, though with smiles in her heart.
pairs.genji_source:
But it was known that
Genji, Tō no Chūjō and Higekuro, the three most powerful men in the
country, were her sponsors; while she was actually attended at the time
of her arrival by Yūgiri and several of her elder brothers, so that
it was by no means likely that she would be overlooked.
pairs.quijote_source:
He himself, persuaded he was conducting the instrument of
his glory, led home by the hand him who had been the utter destruction of
his good name; whom Camilla received with averted countenance, though
with smiles in her heart.

pairs.display: And after some further description of the child’s attainments he proceeded to the Heir Apparent’s and then the third went forward, but with the same result as the first and second.
pairs.genji_half: And after some further description of the child’s attainments he proceeded to the Heir Apparent’s
pairs.quijote_half: and then the third went forward, but with the same result as the first and second.
pairs.genji_source:
And after some
further description of the child’s attainments he proceeded to the Heir
Apparent’s apartments.

There was a certain Tō no Bēn, a son of Kōkiden’s elder brother Tō
Dainagon.
pairs.quijote_source:
Another of my comrades went, and with him the same
happened as with the first, and then the third went forward, but with the
same result as the first and second.

pairs.display: Even among the great nobles and Ministers of the Crown there were some who owed their first promotion to Genji’s good offices; and he by that means took note of the house, and resolved to ascertain with particular care who lived in it.
pairs.genji_half: Even among the great nobles and Ministers of the Crown there were some who owed their first promotion to Genji’s good offices;
pairs.quijote_half: and he by that means took note of the house, and resolved to ascertain with particular care who lived in it.
pairs.genji_source:
Even among the great nobles and
Ministers of the Crown there were some who owed their first promotion
to Genji’s good offices; and countless persons of less importance knew
quite well that they owed everything to him.
pairs.quijote_source:
We pointed out
to him the window at which the reed appeared, and he by that means took
note of the house, and resolved to ascertain with particular care who
lived in it.

pairs.display: Ochiba’s mother now appeared, as if they all had but one throat or were made of sugar paste?
pairs.genji_half: Ochiba’s mother now appeared,
pairs.quijote_half: as if they all had but one throat or were made of sugar paste?
pairs.genji_source:
Ochiba’s
mother now appeared, and Yūgiri hastily moved further away from the
curtains.
pairs.quijote_source:
Is it, pray, any new thing for a single
knight-errant to demolish an army of two hundred thousand men, as if they
all had but one throat or were made of sugar paste?

pairs.display: In due time Princess Chūjō showed them to the Emperor, who was naturally very much interested it plain that their owners were giants, and as tall as great towers; geometry puts this fact beyond a doubt.
pairs.genji_half: In due time Princess Chūjō showed them to the Emperor, who was naturally very much interested
pairs.quijote_half: it plain that their owners were giants, and as tall as great towers; geometry puts this fact beyond a doubt.
pairs.genji_source:
In due time Princess Chūjō
showed them to the Emperor, who was naturally very much interested
and soon afterwards asked for them again, saying that he thought
Princess Akikonomu would like to see them.
pairs.quijote_source:
Likewise, in the
island of Sicily, there have been found leg-bones and arm-bones so large
that their size makes it plain that their owners were giants, and as tall
as great towers; geometry puts this fact beyond a doubt.

pairs.display: At the summons of the herald Genji and Tō no Chūjō now appeared and with them Genji’s half-brother, monsters oppose me; see what hideous countenances come to frighten us!
pairs.genji_half: At the summons of the herald Genji and Tō no Chūjō now appeared and with them Genji’s half-brother,
pairs.quijote_half: monsters oppose me; see what hideous countenances come to frighten us!
pairs.genji_source:
At the summons of the herald Genji and Tō no Chūjō now
appeared and with them Genji’s half-brother, Prince Sochi no Miya,
who among the various arts which he cultivated was particularly fond
of painting.
pairs.quijote_source:
See
what ruffians and villains come out against me; see what monsters oppose
me; see what hideous countenances come to frighten us!

pairs.display: It appeared that even after her partial recovery Genji had continued to be perhaps unduly nervous of leaving her; with as great a retinue of huntsmen and beaters as a crowned king.
pairs.genji_half: It appeared that even after her partial recovery Genji had continued to be perhaps unduly nervous of leaving her;
pairs.quijote_half: with as great a retinue of huntsmen and beaters as a crowned king.
pairs.genji_source:
It appeared that even after her partial recovery
Genji had continued to be perhaps unduly nervous of leaving her; and
this protracted neglect, for which Nyosan would see no adequate cause,
had no doubt tried her patience.
pairs.quijote_source:
Having,
therefore, instructed their servants in everything they were to do, six
days afterwards they took him out to hunt, with as great a retinue of
huntsmen and beaters as a crowned king.

pairs.display: The quiver-bearer’s daughter found and seizing me by the hair he made as though he meant to cut my throat and shear my head clean off.
pairs.genji_half: The quiver-bearer’s daughter found
pairs.quijote_half: and seizing me by the hair he made as though he meant to cut my throat and shear my head clean off.
pairs.genji_source: The quiver-bearer’s daughter found the Emperor still awake.
pairs.quijote_source:
This done, he drew from its
sheath a huge broad scimitar, and seizing me by the hair he made as
though he meant to cut my throat and shear my head clean off.

pairs.display: One day she was as he ran away from his sister, now fully confirmed the truth of what the damsel said.
pairs.genji_half: One day she was
pairs.quijote_half: as he ran away from his sister, now fully confirmed the truth of what the damsel said.
pairs.genji_source:
One day she was
dressing the Emperor’s hair.
pairs.quijote_source:
The appearance of the tipstaffs with her brother in custody, whom one of
them had overtaken as he ran away from his sister, now fully confirmed
the truth of what the damsel said.

pairs.display: To the festival itself however, the anxiety his fainting away had caused them was relieved by his returning to himself.
pairs.genji_half: To the festival itself
pairs.quijote_half: however, the anxiety his fainting away had caused them was relieved by his returning to himself.
pairs.genji_source: To the festival itself Aoi did not go.
pairs.quijote_source:
Those who had been concerned in the joke were now sorry
they had pushed it so far; however, the anxiety his fainting away had
caused them was relieved by his returning to himself.

pairs.display: The old Emperor had constantly invited the widowed lady and her daughter to live with him in the Palace, so much so that, cutting a multitude of capers, they flung their masters to the ground.
pairs.genji_half: The old Emperor had constantly invited the widowed lady and her daughter to live with him in the Palace,
pairs.quijote_half: so much so that, cutting a multitude of capers, they flung their masters to the ground.
pairs.genji_source:
The old Emperor had constantly
invited the widowed lady and her daughter to live with him in the
Palace, but she was reluctant to accept this offer, which indeed was
somewhat impracticable.
pairs.quijote_source:
The poor beasts felt the strange spurs and added to their anguish
by pressing their tails tight, so much so that, cutting a multitude of
capers, they flung their masters to the ground.

pairs.display: There were some papers covered with Genji’s handwriting, and these the old man clasped with an eagerness as was the table, into which it was fitted so exactly that no trace of the joining was visible.
pairs.genji_half: There were some papers covered with Genji’s handwriting, and these the old man clasped with an eagerness
pairs.quijote_half: as was the table, into which it was fitted so exactly that no trace of the joining was visible.
pairs.genji_source:
There were
some papers covered with Genji’s handwriting, and these the old man
clasped with an eagerness that made some of the gentlewomen who had
followed him smile even in the midst of their grief.
pairs.quijote_source:
The head, which resembled a bust
or figure of a Roman emperor, and was coloured like bronze, was hollow
throughout, as was the table, into which it was fitted so exactly that no
trace of the joining was visible.

pairs.display: The Heir Apparent was to have come at the same time as the Emperor; that the poor clown made up his mind to sally forth with him and serve him as esquire.
pairs.genji_half: The Heir Apparent was to have come at the same time as the Emperor;
pairs.quijote_half: that the poor clown made up his mind to sally forth with him and serve him as esquire.
pairs.genji_source:
The Heir Apparent was to have come at
the same time as the Emperor; but it was thought that this arrangement
would be too tiring and the little boy was brought on another day.
pairs.quijote_source:
In a word, he so talked him over, and
with such persuasions and promises, that the poor clown made up his mind
to sally forth with him and serve him as esquire.

pairs.display: It had not very often happened that a collateral descendant of the Emperor was chosen and with tears of joyful satisfaction returned thanks to God our Lord for all his incomparable goodness to us on our voyage.
pairs.genji_half: It had not very often happened that a collateral descendant of the Emperor was chosen
pairs.quijote_half: and with tears of joyful satisfaction returned thanks to God our Lord for all his incomparable goodness to us on our voyage.
pairs.genji_source:
It had not very often happened
that a collateral descendant of the Emperor was chosen for this post;
but on this occasion no other princess of suitable age and lineage was
available.
pairs.quijote_source:
We ran our boat up
on the sand, and all sprang out and kissed the ground, and with tears of
joyful satisfaction returned thanks to God our Lord for all his
incomparable goodness to us on our voyage.

pairs.display: But the Emperor, much as he stood in awe of her, the autumn the winter, and the winter the spring, and so time rolls with never-ceasing wheel.
pairs.genji_half: But the Emperor, much as he stood in awe of her,
pairs.quijote_half: the autumn the winter, and the winter the spring, and so time rolls with never-ceasing wheel.
pairs.genji_source:
But the Emperor, much
as he stood in awe of her, was now obsessed by the idea that if he
again disobeyed the late Emperor’s injunction some terrible calamity
would overtake him.
pairs.quijote_source:
The spring succeeds the
summer, the summer the fall, the fall the autumn, the autumn the winter,
and the winter the spring, and so time rolls with never-ceasing wheel.

pairs.display: The Emperor’s own ladies-in-waiting for having done his duty in the battle and carried off as evidence of his bravery the standard of the Order of Malta.
pairs.genji_half: The Emperor’s own ladies-in-waiting
pairs.quijote_half: for having done his duty in the battle and carried off as evidence of his bravery the standard of the Order of Malta.
pairs.genji_source:
The Emperor’s own
ladies-in-waiting were divided in opinion.
pairs.quijote_source:
They carried me to Constantinople, where the Grand Turk, Selim, made my
master general at sea for having done his duty in the battle and carried
off as evidence of his bravery the standard of the Order of Malta.

pairs.display: At first the Emperor had thought of discussing the matter with the maid Ōmyōbu and asking her and stoutly that the number of the enemy killed in twenty-two general assaults exceeded twenty-five thousand.
pairs.genji_half: At first the Emperor had thought of discussing the matter with the maid Ōmyōbu and asking her
pairs.quijote_half: and stoutly that the number of the enemy killed in twenty-two general assaults exceeded twenty-five thousand.
pairs.genji_source:
At first the Emperor had
thought of discussing the matter with the maid Ōmyōbu and asking her
for a fuller account of his birth and all that had led up to it.
pairs.quijote_source:
The fort also fell; but the Turks had to win it inch by inch,
for the soldiers who defended it fought so gallantly and stoutly that the
number of the enemy killed in twenty-two general assaults exceeded
twenty-five thousand.

pairs.display: Only Tamakatsura and her companions knew that in that rapidly approaching craft there was one who, endeavouring with the utmost persistence to thrust it under his chin, while another kitchen-boy seemed anxious to wash his beard.
pairs.genji_half: Only Tamakatsura and her companions knew that in that rapidly approaching craft there was one who,
pairs.quijote_half: endeavouring with the utmost persistence to thrust it under his chin, while another kitchen-boy seemed anxious to wash his beard.
pairs.genji_source:
Only Tamakatsura and
her companions knew that in that rapidly approaching craft there was
one who, by them at any rate, was far more to be dreaded than the most
ruthless pirate.
pairs.quijote_source:
The one with the trough pursued him and followed
him everywhere he went, endeavouring with the utmost persistence to
thrust it under his chin, while another kitchen-boy seemed anxious to
wash his beard.

pairs.display: But Kojijū began quietly preparing the Princess’s writing things, and presently, with many hesitations, to leave him there to die, as the pain of his wounds would not suffer him to go any farther.
pairs.genji_half: But Kojijū began quietly preparing the Princess’s writing things, and presently, with many hesitations,
pairs.quijote_half: to leave him there to die, as the pain of his wounds would not suffer him to go any farther.
pairs.genji_source:
But Kojijū began quietly
preparing the Princess’s writing things, and presently, with many
hesitations, she produced an answer, which Kojijū under cover of night
managed to convey secretly to Tō no Chūjō’s house.
pairs.quijote_source:
They found Don Vicente in
the arms of his servants, whom he was entreating in a broken feeble voice
to leave him there to die, as the pain of his wounds would not suffer him
to go any farther.

pairs.display: Looking round at Genji’s Fiftieth Day presents which lay about in astonishing profusion she realized how dark and the strange nature of Don Quixote’s madness, and how they were waiting for his squire, who had gone in search of him.
pairs.genji_half: Looking round at Genji’s Fiftieth Day presents which lay about in astonishing profusion she realized how dark
pairs.quijote_half: and the strange nature of Don Quixote’s madness, and how they were waiting for his squire, who had gone in search of him.
pairs.genji_source:
Looking round at Genji’s
Fiftieth Day presents which lay about in astonishing profusion
she realized how dark a day this would have been for her but for the
coming of this messenger from the City.
pairs.quijote_source:
He also explained to them in a few words the
object that had brought them there, and the strange nature of Don
Quixote’s madness, and how they were waiting for his squire, who had gone
in search of him.

pairs.display: Genji had often asked to see them, but hitherto she had refused, and there he would keep us all hanging open-mouthed on the stories he told us of his exploits.
pairs.genji_half: Genji had often asked to see them, but hitherto she had refused,
pairs.quijote_half: and there he would keep us all hanging open-mouthed on the stories he told us of his exploits.
pairs.genji_source:
Genji had often asked to see them, but
hitherto she had refused, and on this occasion it was only in obedience
to her husband’s absolute command that she had brought them to the New
Palace.
pairs.quijote_source:
He used to seat himself on a
bench under the great poplar in our plaza, and there he would keep us all
hanging open-mouthed on the stories he told us of his exploits.

pairs.display: While he wrote to the Lady of his palace and again and her father was horror-stricken, not knowing how willingly she had placed herself in our hands.
pairs.genji_half: While he wrote to the Lady of his palace and again
pairs.quijote_half: and her father was horror-stricken, not knowing how willingly she had placed herself in our hands.
pairs.genji_source:
While he wrote to
the Lady of his palace and again while he wrote to Fujitsubo in her
cloister he wept so bitterly that the letters had many times to be put
aside.
pairs.quijote_source:
When his daughter caught sight of him she covered her eyes so as
not to see him, and her father was horror-stricken, not knowing how
willingly she had placed herself in our hands.

pairs.display: It was on his conscience that he had not for a long while been to the Great Hall; but he would not on any account, saying that modesty became knights-errant just as much as valour.
pairs.genji_half: It was on his conscience that he had not for a long while been to the Great Hall;
pairs.quijote_half: but he would not on any account, saying that modesty became knights-errant just as much as valour.
pairs.genji_source:
It was on his
conscience that he had not for a long while been to the Great Hall;
but fearing that Murasaki too might be feeling very unhappy he first
went home to give her her lessons.
pairs.quijote_source:
They asked him to let himself be stripped that they
might put a shirt on him, but he would not on any account, saying that
modesty became knights-errant just as much as valour.

pairs.display: But now that he saw how astonishingly the one resembled the other he fancied that all the while Murasaki had who have gained by war fall short of the number of those who have perished in it?
pairs.genji_half: But now that he saw how astonishingly the one resembled the other he fancied that all the while Murasaki had
pairs.quijote_half: who have gained by war fall short of the number of those who have perished in it?
pairs.genji_source:
But now that he saw how astonishingly the one resembled
the other he fancied that all the while Murasaki had but served as a
substitute or eidolon of the lady who denied him her love.
pairs.quijote_source:
For tell me,
sirs, if you have ever reflected upon it, by how much do those who have
gained by war fall short of the number of those who have perished in it?

pairs.display: During the first three nights Murasaki saw nothing of him, and though this was quite natural under the circumstances, her instead of treating her as guilty in the way that may seem best to thee?
pairs.genji_half: During the first three nights Murasaki saw nothing of him, and though this was quite natural under the circumstances,
pairs.quijote_half: her instead of treating her as guilty in the way that may seem best to thee?
pairs.quijote_source:
If thou dost
not hold her to be what thou why dost thou seek to prove her instead of
treating her as guilty in the way that may seem best to thee?

pairs.display: I ought to have told you that before his departure he sent a message to Ise for, after trial, it will but be in the same estimation as before.
pairs.genji_half: I ought to have told you that before his departure he sent a message to Ise
pairs.quijote_half: for, after trial, it will but be in the same estimation as before.
pairs.genji_source:
I ought to have told you that before his departure
he sent a message to Ise with a letter informing Lady Rokujō of the
place at which she must in future address him.
pairs.quijote_source:
but if
she be as virtuous as thou believest, it is an uncalled-for proceeding
to make trial of truth itself, for, after trial, it will but be in the
same estimation as before.

pairs.display: But Yūgiri could not summon up much interest in the round of visits upon which his father had embarked, the said Vicente having returned from service as a soldier in Italy and divers other parts.
pairs.genji_half: But Yūgiri could not summon up much interest in the round of visits upon which his father had embarked,
pairs.quijote_half: the said Vicente having returned from service as a soldier in Italy and divers other parts.
pairs.genji_source:
But Yūgiri could not summon up much interest in the round of visits
upon which his father had embarked, and slipped away to the rooms of
his little sister, the Princess from Akashi.
pairs.quijote_source:
About this time there arrived in our town one Vicente de la Roca, the son
of a poor peasant of the same town, the said Vicente having returned from
service as a soldier in Italy and divers other parts.

pairs.display: Duly crowned, Genji went to his chamber and changing into man’s dress went down into the courtyard or thou knowest not what thou dost demand.
pairs.genji_half: Duly crowned, Genji went to his chamber and changing into man’s dress went down into the courtyard
pairs.quijote_half: or thou knowest not what thou dost demand.
pairs.genji_source:
Duly crowned, Genji went to his chamber and changing into man’s dress
went down into the courtyard and performed the Dance of Homage, which
he did with such grace that tears stood in every eye.
pairs.quijote_source:
Either thou dost not hold her to be what
thou sayest, or thou knowest not what thou dost demand.

pairs.display: It is on this day that the fifth book is read; despite the disapproval of Kōkiden and had been for ages lying forgotten in a corner eaten with rust and covered with mildew.
pairs.genji_half: It is on this day that the fifth book is read; despite the disapproval of Kōkiden
pairs.quijote_half: and had been for ages lying forgotten in a corner eaten with rust and covered with mildew.
pairs.genji_source:
It is on this
day that the fifth book is read; despite the disapproval of Kōkiden
and her flatterers, the ceremony was attended by the greater
part of those about the Court.
pairs.quijote_source:
The first thing he did was to clean up some armour that had belonged to
his great-grandfather, and had been for ages lying forgotten in a corner
eaten with rust and covered with mildew.

pairs.display: But Lady Yūgao died who lived in the stalls of Sanchobienaya, and that wherever she might be she would serve and esteem him as her lord.
pairs.genji_half: But Lady Yūgao died
pairs.quijote_half: who lived in the stalls of Sanchobienaya, and that wherever she might be she would serve and esteem him as her lord.
pairs.genji_source: But Lady Yūgao died a long while ago.’
pairs.quijote_source:
She
answered with great humility that she was called La Tolosa, and that she
was the daughter of a cobbler of Toledo who lived in the stalls of
Sanchobienaya, and that wherever she might be she would serve and esteem
him as her lord.

pairs.display: Genji, who considered the weather too hot it seemed as though the two valiant and wrathful combatants stood threatening heaven, and earth, and hell, with such resolution and determination did they bear themselves.
pairs.genji_half: Genji, who considered the weather too hot
pairs.quijote_half: it seemed as though the two valiant and wrathful combatants stood threatening heaven, and earth, and hell, with such resolution and determination did they bear themselves.
pairs.genji_source:
Genji, who considered the
weather too hot for visits, frowned, at which the ladies-in-waiting
tittered.
pairs.quijote_source:
To be brief, its Second Part, according to the translation,
began in this way:

With trenchant swords upraised and poised on high, it seemed as though
the two valiant and wrathful combatants stood threatening heaven, and
earth, and hell, with such resolution and determination did they bear
themselves.

pairs.display: Genji, making furious signs at them to be quiet, and with them they put down a half cheese harder than if it had been made of mortar.
pairs.genji_half: Genji, making furious signs at them to be quiet,
pairs.quijote_half: and with them they put down a half cheese harder than if it had been made of mortar.
pairs.genji_source:
Genji, making furious signs at them to be quiet, flung
himself on to a divan.
pairs.quijote_source:
The course of meat finished, they spread upon the
sheepskins a great heap of parched acorns, and with them they put down a
half cheese harder than if it had been made of mortar.

pairs.display: Genji, quietly surveying the scene, decided this was one of those and ivy, wherewith they went as bravely and becomingly decked as our Court dames with all the rare and far-fetched artifices that idle curiosity has taught them.
pairs.genji_half: Genji, quietly surveying the scene, decided this was one of those
pairs.quijote_half: and ivy, wherewith they went as bravely and becomingly decked as our Court dames with all the rare and far-fetched artifices that idle curiosity has taught them.
pairs.genji_source:
Genji, quietly surveying the scene, decided this was
one of those middle-class families which in last night’s conversation
had been so highly commended.
pairs.quijote_source:
Nor were their ornaments like those in use to-day,
set off by Tyrian purple, and silk tortured in endless fashions, but the
wreathed leaves of the green dock and ivy, wherewith they went as bravely
and becomingly decked as our Court dames with all the rare and
far-fetched artifices that idle curiosity has taught them.

pairs.display: Genji began to doze, and waited till she was somewhat more advanced in age and could mate herself to her own liking.
pairs.genji_half: Genji began to doze,
pairs.quijote_half: and waited till she was somewhat more advanced in age and could mate herself to her own liking.
pairs.genji_source:
Genji began to doze, and his attendants sat
silent and motionless.
pairs.quijote_source:
At these, to all
appearance, reasonable excuses that she made, her uncle ceased to urge
her, and waited till she was somewhat more advanced in age and could mate
herself to her own liking.

pairs.display: Genji was rather disappointed that she for the sufferings of her neighbours, so she at once set about tending Don Quixote, and made her young daughter, a very comely girl, help her in taking care of her guest.
pairs.genji_half: Genji was rather disappointed that she
pairs.quijote_half: for the sufferings of her neighbours, so she at once set about tending Don Quixote, and made her young daughter, a very comely girl, help her in taking care of her guest.
pairs.genji_source:
Genji was rather disappointed that she did not ask more
questions about him.
pairs.quijote_source:
The innkeeper
had a wife whose disposition was not such as those of her calling
